PMC10999549.diagnosis: Gallbladder volvulus (torsion) causing ischemic/gangrenous acalculous cholecystitis
rationale_summary: Previously healthy 5-year-old with acute epigastric/RLQ pain and vomiting, positive Murphy's sign, no jaundice and normal liver/biliary labs

Ultrasound: enlarged gallbladder with wall thickening, pericholecystic fluid/fat stranding and no gallstones; repeat US showed absent gallbladder wall vascular flow and a cone-shaped structure at the neck with fundus displaced left and mobile with repositioning
CT: fundus deviation from gallbladder bed, irregular poorly enhancing wall with suspected intraluminal hemorrhage and pericholecystic inflammatory changes, supporting torsion with ischemia/necrosis

PMC11535749.diagnosis: SBP-101-induced bilateral toxic maculopathy (progressive outer retinopathy/RPE atrophy)
rationale_summary: Normal baseline eye exam/OCT before SBP-101, then VA decline after the second monthly cycle with new macular pigmentary changes and ellipsoid zone disruption

Progressive bilateral RPE and outer retinal atrophy with sub-RPE deposits, hypoautofluorescent macular atrophy, central scotomas, photopsias, and choroidal thinning despite stopping drug and intravitreal steroids
ERG shows relatively preserved rod function with cone-predominant dysfunction (reduced 30 Hz flicker), supporting toxic outer retinopathy
Paraneoplastic retinopathy workup (anti-recoverin/anti-enolase) was negative and she lacked diabetes/hypertension to explain findings

PMC11821356.diagnosis: Adult-onset PFAPA syndrome (Periodic Fever, Aphthous stomatitis, Pharyngitis, and Cervical Adenitis)
rationale_summary: Recurrent, predictable febrile episodes every 6-8 weeks lasting up to 7 days with complete well-being between attacks
Attacks consistently include sore throat/pharyngitis with white exudate and painful cervical/submandibular lymphadenopathy
Inflammatory markers markedly elevated during episodes (CRP 64 mg/L, SAA 140 mg/L) with normal procalcitonin
Extensive infectious workup negative (HIV/CMV/EBV, cultures no growth) and no response to multiple antibiotic courses
Lymph node biopsy showed reactive hyperplasia and bone marrow/cytogenetics were unremarkable, supporting an autoinflammatory periodic fever syndrome

PMC10622404.diagnosis: Severe rhabdomyolysis causing myoglobinuric acute kidney injury with life-threatening hyperkalemia and metabolic acidosis
rationale_summary: Several days of progressive diffuse myalgia and profound weakness/inability to walk
Marked rhabdomyolysis labs: creatine phosphokinase >22,000 U/L and myoglobin >30,000 μg/L with very high AST/LDH
Acute kidney failure (BUN 304 mg/dL, creatinine 6.58 mg/dL) superimposed on CKD
Severe hyperkalemia (K 7.5 mmol/L) with metabolic acidosis (pH 7.26) and ECG changes (peaked T waves, bradyarrhythmia requiring temporary pacing)

PMC9675078.diagnosis: Epstein-Barr virus infectious mononucleosis presenting as preseptal inflammation (dacryoadenitis/dacryocystitis) mimicking a periorbital abscess

PMC3747369.diagnosis: Nevirapine-induced hypersensitivity reaction (DRESS/drug hypersensitivity syndrome) with acute hepatitis and acute kidney injury
rationale_summary: Symptoms began ~20 days after starting nevirapine (recent regimen change 45 days prior)
High fever and diffuse maculopapular rash involving >50% body surface area
Marked jaundice with choluria, pruritus, RUQ pain and elevated AST/ALT, bilirubin, ALP, GGT consistent with drug-induced hepatitis/cholestatic injury
Acute kidney injury with ~3-fold creatinine rise accompanying systemic reaction
Infectious hepatitis causes were not found (HAV IgM, HBV/HCV antibodies, CMV antigenemia negative)

PMC10216786.diagnosis: Hyperventilation-induced severe hypophosphatemia causing acute hypophosphatemic paralysis (with rhabdomyolysis)
rationale_summary: Two acute episodes of tetraparesis occurred in the setting of marked hyperventilation with hypocapnia/respiratory alkalosis (pH 7.57, PaCO₂ 19.2 mmHg)
Both episodes showed severe hypophosphatemia (0.2 mmol/L in the second episode) with no evidence of renal phosphate wasting
Neuromuscular workup was unrevealing (normal MRI, nerve conduction studies/myography; negative myasthenia testing; hypokalemic periodic paralysis ruled out by exercise tests and genetic analysis)
Second episode included tetany/Trousseau's sign and marked rhabdomyolysis (CK 44,913 U/L), consistent with severe hypophosphatemia-related muscle dysfunction/injury

PMC7325212.diagnosis: Distal hereditary motor neuropathy (length-dependent axonal motor polyneuropathy presenting with bilateral foot drop)
rationale_summary: 5-year slowly progressive, length-dependent distal leg weakness (bilateral foot drop) with preserved proximal and upper-limb strength
Areflexia at knees/ankles with minimal sensory loss and normal sensory nerve conduction studies (motor-predominant process)
Motor NCS: mildly reduced peroneal/tibial CMAP amplitudes with normal latencies/velocities (axonal motor neuropathy pattern)
Needle EMG: distal denervation/chronic reinnervation (TA/gastrocnemius) with normal proximal/upper-limb muscles, supporting a length-dependent motor neuropathy
Extensive acquired neuropathy workup and CMT panel/DM1-2 testing were negative, favoring a distal motor neuropathy phenotype despite incidental myotonic discharges

PMC6815408.diagnosis: Relapsing-remitting multiple sclerosis (pediatric-onset MS) with bilateral PPRF (horizontal gaze) involvement
rationale_summary: Multiple CNS demyelinating-appearing lesions on MRI (periventricular/subcortical brain lesions plus cervical and thoracic spinal cord lesions)
Clinical episodes separated in time (hand paresthesia 1 year prior; ascending sensory symptoms 2 months prior; acute brainstem ocular motor deficit now)
Brainstem localization supported by bilateral pontine PPRF hyperintensities corresponding to complete bilateral horizontal gaze palsy with preserved vertical gaze
Evidence of active demyelination with gadolinium enhancement of the T4 spinal lesion
Anti-aquaporin-4 and anti-MOG antibodies negative and visual evoked potentials normal, making NMOSD/MOGAD and optic pathway involvement less likely

PMC9610036.diagnosis: Primary central nervous system lymphoma (with leptomeningeal/dural involvement)
rationale_summary: Initial noncontrast CT showed a hyperdense suprasellar mass, a typical appearance for CNS lymphoma
Brain MRI demonstrated multiple enhancing lesions in suprasellar, pineal, and periatrial/periventricular regions with rapid interval enlargement
Evolving cranial neuropathy (progressive right third nerve palsy) and new focal dural thickening support infiltrative neoplastic meningeal involvement

CSF showed lymphocytic pleocytosis, elevated protein, and elevated IgG index with negative infectious/inflammatory testing and no malignant cells on two taps (CSF cytology can be negative in CNS lymphoma)

PMC9246066.diagnosis: Food protein-induced allergic proctocolitis (cow's milk protein intolerance) mimicking necrotizing enterocolitis
rationale_summary: Developed hematochezia shortly after initiation of breast milk feeds
Persistent leukocytosis with marked eosinophilia (14%)
Family history of milk-protein intolerance with hematochezia after milk exposure
Imaging showed rectosigmoid-predominant bowel-wall thickening/hyperenhancement without pneumatosis, portal venous gas, or free air, arguing against NEC

PMC3884960.diagnosis: Primary clear cell carcinoma (Müllerian-type clear cell adenocarcinoma) of the renal pelvis
rationale_summary: Tumor diffusely replaces the renal pelvicalyceal lining with papillary/solid nodules composed of clear and hobnail cells with focal psammoma bodies and hyaline basement membrane-like cores
Immunophenotype supports Müllerian-type clear cell carcinoma: CK7+, HNF-1β+, PAX8+, focal CA-125+
Excludes conventional urothelial carcinoma: uroplakin III−, p63−, CK20− and no urothelial in situ/invasive component identified
Argues against clear cell renal cell carcinoma involving the pelvis: RCC marker antigen− and CD10− despite PAX8 positivity

PMC4012653.diagnosis: Wernicke encephalopathy (thiamine deficiency precipitated by glucose/refeeding in severe malnutrition)
rationale_summary: Profound malnutrition and weight loss (BMI 14.5) from lymphoma-associated anorexia/depression
Received prolonged IV dextrose and nutritional supplementation before neurologic decline, a setting that can precipitate thiamine-deficiency encephalopathy
Acute encephalopathy with ataxia, agitation/visual hallucinations and later drowsiness/hypothermia, with negative head CT and repeated negative infectious work-up
Severe hypomagnesemia (0.34 mmol/L), which can contribute to/impair correction of thiamine-related neurologic dysfunction

PMC9176094.diagnosis: Autosomal recessive pseudohypoaldosteronism type 1 (systemic mineralocorticoid resistance due to ENaC defect)
rationale_summary: Severe neonatal salt-wasting crises starting at 6 days with hyponatremia, hyperkalemia (K up to 10 mmol/L), and metabolic acidosis requiring resuscitation/dialysis
Normal female genitalia with 46,XX karyotype and SRY negative; imaging normal
Repeat hormonal evaluation was inconsistent with congenital adrenal hyperplasia
Recurrent episodes and inadequate clinical response despite hydrocortisone and fludrocortisone (suggesting mineralocorticoid resistance rather than deficiency)
Consanguineous parents support an autosomal recessive disorder

PMC7845154.diagnosis: Isolated left cervical/supraclavicular lymph node metastasis from recurrent caecal (colorectal) adenocarcinoma
rationale_summary: History of poorly differentiated caecal adenocarcinoma treated with surgery and incomplete adjuvant therapy
New rapidly enlarging left level IV neck node with malignant ultrasound features (loss of hilum, increased vascularity, intranodal calcifications)
FNA shows atypical malignant cells; immunohistochemistry positive for cytokeratin AE1/AE3 and CDX2 supporting gastrointestinal/colorectal origin
Elevated carcinoembryonic antigen (24.0 mg/L) consistent with colorectal cancer recurrence
PET-CT shows FDG-avid left level IV and supraclavicular nodes with no other thoracic/abdominal disease, fitting nodal metastatic recurrence

PMC6548910.diagnosis: Sclerosing angiomatoid nodular transformation (SANT) of the spleen
rationale_summary: Incidentally detected, well-circumscribed 40-mm splenic mass in an asymptomatic 41-year-old woman with normal labs and tumor markers
Dynamic CT/MRI showed relatively low early enhancement with slow centripetal progressive enhancement becoming isodense/isointense to the spleen on delayed imaging (10 minutes)
MRI signal was isointense to spleen on both T1- and T2-weighted images, consistent with a benign fibrosing/vascular splenic lesion pattern
PET-CT showed no significant FDG uptake, arguing against splenic lymphoma or metastasis and supporting a benign lesion such as SANT

PMC11447760.diagnosis: Non-islet cell tumor hypoglycemia (paraneoplastic IGF-2-mediated hypoglycemia from metastatic myoepithelial carcinoma)

Cortisol (23) not low, making adrenal insufficiency unlikely

rationale_summary: 16-year-old with large intramuscular posteroinferior thigh mass
Initial FNA showed rhabdomyoblastic spindle/ovoid cells; immunopositive for desmin and myogenin, with no lipogenic differentiation identified
FOXO1 (13q14) rearrangement by FISH negative, supporting embryonal rather than alveolar rhabdomyosarcoma

PMC7676809.diagnosis: Distal ureterolithiasis (ureteral stone at the pelvic brim crossing the iliac vessels)
rationale_summary: RLQ pain with nausea but normal WBC and CRP argues against appendicitis/inflammatory bowel pathology
Ultrasound shows a hyperechoic circular focus with posterior acoustic shadowing consistent with a calculus
The calculus is within a non-peristalsing, non-compressible, thin-walled tubular structure adjacent to the iliac vessels, matching the distal ureter location where it crosses the iliac vessels
Absent mesenteric fat stranding, free fluid, hyperemia, and lack of typical layered bowel wall appearance further support ureter (not bowel/appendix) as the structure involved

rationale_summary: Subacute migratory, nonerosive polyarthritis (ankles, knees, hips, wrists, small hand joints) with elevated ESR/CRP and poor response to NSAIDs

PMC5412606.diagnosis: Typical AV nodal re-entrant tachycardia (slow-fast AVNRT)

Baseline study showed no manifest or concealed accessory pathway conduction, making orthodromic AVRT less likely

PMC10586915.diagnosis: Late bioprosthetic aortic valve thrombosis with cardioembolic stroke
rationale_summary: Transoesophageal echocardiography showed a 10×7.5 mm mobile echogenic mass attached to the bioprosthetic aortic valve with new dysfunction (moderate stenosis, severe paravalvular regurgitation, leaflet degeneration).

No clinical or laboratory evidence of infective endocarditis, supporting procedure-related mechanical valve injury

PMC4389205.diagnosis: Paradoxical cryptococcal immune reconstitution inflammatory syndrome (IRIS) with intra-abdominal involvement (ileocecal/appendicular inflammatory mass and lymphadenopathy)
rationale_summary: Recent treated cryptococcal meningitis followed by ART initiation, with new inflammatory symptoms beginning ~21 days after ART start
Temporal immune change after ART (CD4 rose from 5 to 29 cells/µL by day +21) coinciding with onset of abdominal pain, low-grade fevers and cough
Progressive imaging abnormalities (appendicular mass, para-aortic/adjacent lymphadenopathy, ileal wall thickening) despite unremarkable initial studies and lack of response to antibacterial therapy
Biopsied lymph nodes showed no alternative histologic diagnosis, and empiric antibiotics did not resolve the abdominal process

PMC4637487.diagnosis: Systemic AL (light-chain) amyloidosis causing bilateral phrenic axonal neuropathy/diaphragmatic weakness with restrictive ventilatory failure
rationale_summary: Known IgG-kappa multiple myeloma with markedly increased kappa free light chains (K-FLC 1379 mg/dL; K/L ratio 2121), a major risk for AL amyloidosis
Progressive dyspnea with hypercapnic respiratory failure (pCO2 60 mmHg) and restrictive PFTs (FVC 58%, preserved FEV1/FVC)
Nerve conduction study showed bilateral phrenic axonal neuropathy, explaining ventilatory failure

Alternative cardiopulmonary causes were not supported: V/Q scan negative for PE, preserved LV size/EF, and right heart catheterization after diuresis showed normal wedge pressure (10 mmHg) without significant pulmonary hypertension (34/9/18 mmHg)

PMC5054479.diagnosis: Congenital insensitivity to pain with anhidrosis (hereditary sensory and autonomic neuropathy type IV, CIPA)
rationale_summary: Onset in infancy with recurrent unexplained fevers and absence of sweating (anhidrosis).
Severe self-mutilation starting with teething (biting fingers/toes, tongue/skin injuries) consistent with absent pain sensation.
Repeated trauma with multiple fractures, recurrent joint dislocations requiring surgery, progressive limb deformities, chronic ulcers and osteomyelitis leading to amputations.
Consanguineous parents supports an autosomal-recessive hereditary sensory/autonomic neuropathy rather than Lesch-Nyhan (also supported by normal uric acid).

PMC11079415.diagnosis: Delayed thrombosis of the residual vein of Galen aneurysmal sac after embolisation
rationale_summary: Known mural-type vein of Galen aneurysmal malformation treated with transarterial Onyx embolisation achieving venous sac occlusion

PMC3961933.diagnosis: Cemento-ossifying fibroma of the mandible (giant ossifying/cementifying fibroma)
rationale_summary: 6-year history of slow-growing, painless, hard mandibular swelling with marked but noninvasive expansion

Imaging shows a well-defined, mixed radiolucent-radiopaque lesion with "cotton-wool" calcifications and characteristic concentric/centrifugal ("ball-like") growth causing bowing (not erosion) of the inferior border
CT confirms a large, well-circumscribed expansile mandibular lesion with mixed sclerotic and lucent areas
Normal serum calcium/phosphorus/alkaline phosphatase supports a localized benign fibro-osseous tumor rather than a metabolic bone disorder

PMC3892129.diagnosis: Pituitary abscess complicating a giant invasive prolactinoma with secondary bacterial meningitis
rationale_summary: Marked hyperprolactinemia (PRL 9000 ng/mL) with panhypopituitarism and 2-year progressive visual loss suggests a large prolactin-secreting pituitary macroadenoma
CT/MRI show a large sellar/suprasellar mass eroding the sellar floor and extending into the sphenoid sinus/cavernous sinuses with optic chiasm compression
Acute severe headache/vomiting with fever, very high CRP and leukocytosis indicate an infectious process

Lumbar puncture shows purulent CSF (11,900 WBC/µL, very high protein, low glucose, high lactate) consistent with bacterial meningitis, fitting contiguous spread/abscess related to the invasive sellar lesion
MRI reports no intratumoral hemorrhage, arguing against pituitary apoplexy as the cause of the acute presentation

PMC8367851.diagnosis: Systemic pyoderma gangrenosum (neutrophilic disease) with sterile pulmonary and splenic abscesses (aseptic abscess syndrome)
rationale_summary: History of recurrent biopsy-proven pyoderma gangrenosum with dense neutrophilic infiltrate and negative bacterial/AFB/fungal studies
Developed multiple cavitary lung lesions, splenic abscesses, and pericardial effusion while afebrile with neutrophilic leukocytosis
Extensive pulmonary sampling and two wedge resections showed abscess formation but no organisms by stains, cultures, or molecular studies
Clinical deterioration after stopping immunosuppression and lack of improvement with broad-spectrum antibacterial/antifungal therapy support a sterile neutrophilic process rather than infection
ANCA negative, arguing against ANCA-associated vasculitis as a cause of cavitary lung disease

PMC6544423.diagnosis: Bilateral coronary-subclavian steal syndrome from progression to critical bilateral subclavian artery stenosis/occlusion compromising LIMA/RIMA coronary bypass graft flow
rationale_summary: Prior CABG used both internal mammary arteries (LIMA and RIMA), making coronary perfusion dependent on subclavian inflow
Known bilateral subclavian artery stenosis (50%) on prior MR angiography

Progressive very low/difficult-to-measure bilateral arm blood pressures with easily palpable femoral pulses suggests pseudo-hypotension from severe proximal subclavian disease rather than true systemic hypotension
Developed ischemic findings (diffuse ST-segment depression, positive troponin, rising CPK) with new LV systolic dysfunction (EF fell to 40%)
Presented with acute cardiogenic pulmonary edema and low arm pressures consistent with myocardial ischemia precipitated by reduced subclavian/IMA graft flow (coronary-subclavian steal)

PMC11282747.diagnosis: Scrub typhus (Orientia tsutsugamushi infection) complicated by septic shock/ARDS and intracerebral hemorrhage
rationale_summary: Acute febrile illness in a woman from rural Nepal with a characteristic brownish-black eschar on the buttock

Multiorgan involvement consistent with scrub typhus: hypotensive shock, hypoxemia with bilateral pulmonary infiltrates/edema, AKI (creatinine 1.7 mg/dL), and hepatitis/cholestasis with jaundice (bilirubin 6.2 mg/dL, AST/ALT elevated, ALP elevated)
Alternative infectious causes were investigated and were negative (dengue, leptospirosis, brucellosis, malaria smear, SARS‑CoV‑2 PCR, TB studies, HIV)
Neurologic deficit with CT showing acute right frontal intraparenchymal hemorrhage, a reported complication of scrub typhus-related vasculitis/endothelial injury

PMC8577092.diagnosis: Cerebral salt-wasting syndrome (post-intracerebral hemorrhage) causing hypovolemic shock
rationale_summary: Recent spontaneous intracerebral hemorrhage followed by several days of marked polyuria and 6-kg weight loss
Clear hypovolemia/shock: hypotension 74/30 mmHg, tachycardia, mottling/cool extremities, dry mucosa/skin-fold dehydration, collapsible IVC; improved with saline
Hypotonic hyponatremia (Na 123 mmol/L, plasma osmolality 254) with inappropriately high urine sodium and chloride (Na 62 mmol/L, Cl 64 mmol/L) indicating renal salt loss rather than extrarenal losses
Hemoconcentration and prerenal azotemia (elevated urea/creatinine) support volume depletion rather than SIADH

rationale_summary: Sudden severe abdominal pain with rigid abdomen and rebound tenderness (acute peritonitis)

rationale_summary: Prenatal ultrasound showed organized fetal parts within a retroperitoneal mass (skin-covered limb, long bone, footpad/toe-like echoes; buttock-like contour).
Two fetal-like components were identified, one with faint heartbeat and movements, consistent with a parasitic twin rather than a simple teratoma.
Doppler demonstrated vascular connection with blood supply to the live mass arising from the host fetus's abdominal aorta, supporting fetus-in-fetu.
Mass effect with displacement/compression of major vessels and organs (IVC/aorta shift, bowel and kidney displacement) consistent with a sizable intra-abdominal parasitic mass.

PMC6709319.diagnosis: Bucillamine-induced interstitial lung disease presenting as organizing pneumonia superimposed on pre-existing usual interstitial pneumonia (RA-associated UIP)
rationale_summary: Dyspnea developed after starting bucillamine for newly diagnosed rheumatoid arthritis
HRCT shows new ground-glass opacities and consolidations superimposed on chronic UIP findings (honeycombing/traction bronchiectasis), with acute changes sparing the lung bases
BAL shows inflammatory alveolitis with mixed lymphocytes/neutrophils/eosinophils and negative cultures, arguing against infection
Transbronchial biopsy demonstrates organizing pneumonia with septal thickening and inflammatory cell infiltration, a pattern consistent with drug-induced lung injury

PMC3564269.diagnosis: Neurosarcoidosis with neuromuscular involvement (sarcoid-related cranial neuropathies, polyneuropathy, and inflammatory myopathy)
rationale_summary: History of biopsy-proven sarcoidosis with current pulmonary abnormalities (multiple calcified nodules and new right lower lobe infiltrate) plus weight loss and chronic cough
Multiple cranial neuropathies (bilateral III and VI palsies with ptosis) with subtle facial weakness/sensory loss, compatible with neurosarcoidosis
Diffuse predominantly axonal length-dependent sensory-motor polyneuropathy with areflexia and CSF albuminocytologic dissociation

Muscle biopsy showed inflammatory myopathy (fiber atrophy/regeneration) and sural nerve biopsy suggested prior vasculitic-type injury, supporting systemic inflammatory sarcoid-related neuromuscular disease after negative infectious workup and unrevealing brain MRI/MRA

PMC8377818.diagnosis: Primary high-grade neuroendocrine carcinoma (small cell carcinoma) of the pineal region
rationale_summary: Pineal-region obstructing mass with highly cellular primitive round-cell morphology, brisk mitoses (20/10 HPF) and very high Ki-67 (60-70%) consistent with high-grade malignancy

Strong neuroendocrine differentiation (synaptophysin and NSE strongly positive; focal chromogranin and neurofilament) with diffuse cytokeratin expression (CK8/18, AE1/AE3, CAM5.2) supports neuroendocrine carcinoma

Excludes major mimics by immunophenotype: GFAP negative (not glial), EMA negative (argues against papillary tumor of pineal region), INI1 retained (not ATRT), TTF-1 negative and PET/CT without other FDG-avid disease (no clear extracranial primary)

Secondary amenorrhea with hypogonadotropic hypogonadism (FSH 1.4, LH 0.4 mIU/mL) indicates central pituitary/hypothalamic dysfunction
Thyroid axis pattern (low TSH 0.209 with low free T3 and non-elevated free T4) supports central hypothyroidism
Hyperprolactinemia (prolactin 90.8 ng/mL) suggests pituitary stalk effect or pituitary tumor contributing to hypopituitarism

PMC3934446.diagnosis: Intracranial germinoma (bifocal suprasellar/pituitary stalk-hypothalamic and basal ganglia/internal capsule involvement)
rationale_summary: Adolescent male with central diabetes insipidus (polyuria, low urine osmolality, hypernatremia) and panhypopituitarism
MRI shows homogeneously enhancing hypothalamic/pituitary mass with marked stalk thickening and absent posterior pituitary bright spot
Progressive hemiparesis/dystonia with T2 hyperintensity in basal ganglia/internal capsule and ipsilateral cerebral peduncle atrophy, a pattern consistent with basal ganglia germinoma
CSF β-hCG is slightly elevated and α-fetoprotein is negative, supporting germinoma over other stalk lesions
Alternative causes of stalk thickening (sarcoidosis, Langerhans cell histiocytosis) are not supported by normal ACE, normal chest CT, and no lytic bone lesions

PMC8168026.diagnosis: Congenital lower esophageal sphincter achalasia (esophagogastric junction outflow obstruction) with secondary generalized megaesophagus
rationale_summary: 7-month-old kitten with severe postprandial regurgitation and weight loss despite normal appetite
Thoracic radiographs/contrast show severe diffuse esophageal dilatation consistent with generalized megaesophagus

Persistent contrast/aeric distension with apparent lack of passage/continuity between distal esophagus and stomach suggests functional obstruction at the gastroesophageal junction rather than focal cranial lesion
No evidence of aspiration pneumonia or systemic illness to suggest another primary cause

PMC9006327.diagnosis: Calcified amorphous tumor (CAT) of the left atrium
rationale_summary: Transthoracic echocardiography and contrast CT showed a 39 mm left atrial mass adherent to the atrial septum with partial calcification
Brain MRI demonstrated minuscule scattered cerebral infarctions consistent with embolization from an intracardiac mass
Lack of valvular abnormalities and negative blood cultures make infective endocarditis less likely despite fever, supporting a non-neoplastic calcified intracardiac mass mimicking myxoma

PMC8233074.diagnosis: Infected enlarged prostatic utricle (Müllerian duct remnant) presenting as UTI-like symptoms
rationale_summary: Mixed gonadal dysgenesis and history of proximal hypospadias repair are risk factors for a prostatic utricle remnant/enlargement
Persistent suprapubic/RLQ pain, dysuria, urgency/frequency, foul-smelling urine, and small urethral discharge despite antibiotics suggest an anatomic/inflammatory source rather than simple cystitis
Marked pyuria on repeated urinalyses with an initial urine culture showing no growth and elevated CRP supports sterile inflammation/occult infection in a pelvic remnant
Ultrasound description of rectal wall thickening with an air-fluid level is consistent with a pelvic midline cystic process (e.g., enlarged/infected utricle) that can mimic rectal pathology

PMC11624566.diagnosis: Primary intrahepatic ectopic thyroid carcinoma (thyroid follicular carcinoma arising in the liver)
rationale_summary: Solitary enlarging quadrate liver lobe mass with epithelial neoplasia on cytology and progressive growth on imaging
Histopathology shows follicular structures with dense eosinophilic colloid-like material and areas of tubular/solid proliferation with mitoses, consistent with thyroid-type carcinoma
Neoplastic cells show diffuse cytoplasmic thyroglobulin immunopositivity, indicating thyroid follicular differentiation

No CT evidence of an orthotopic thyroid mass or other primary tumor elsewhere, and T4/TSH within reference intervals, supporting ectopic thyroid origin rather than metastatic functional thyroid disease

PMC6047986.diagnosis: Idiopathic multicentric Castleman disease (IL-6-driven lymphoproliferative disorder) presenting with protein-losing enteropathy/intestinal involvement
rationale_summary: Marked systemic inflammation and cytokine activation (CRP 2.75 mg/dL, IL-6 20.7 pg/mL, soluble IL-2 receptor 1,950 U/mL) with severe anemia and hypoalbuminemia/low total protein

Imaging showed intra-abdominal lymphadenopathy with small-intestinal mucosal thickening; endoscopy showed abnormal shaggy yellow-white duodenal mucosa consistent with intestinal involvement causing protein loss

Constitutional symptoms (3-year anorexia, malaise, 15 kg weight loss/cachexia) and clinical improvement on systemic corticosteroids with relapse after taper/withdrawal fit an IL-6-mediated multicentric process

PMC10689293.diagnosis: Suprasellar (parasellar) neurocysticercosis causing hypopituitarism/secondary adrenal insufficiency
rationale_summary: Born in and frequently travels to Honduras (endemic area for Taenia solium)
MRI shows a partially calcified suprasellar/parasellar mass with cystic and solid components, compatible with a calcified/degenerating cysticercus lesion in the sellar region
Progressive headaches/nausea and decreased peripheral vision consistent with mass effect at the suprasellar region/optic chiasm
Low morning cortisol with low ACTH indicates secondary adrenal insufficiency from pituitary-hypothalamic involvement by the sellar/suprasellar lesion

rationale_summary: Incidental, asymptomatic, well-circumscribed intraventricular mass in a 59-year-old (typical for benign subependymoma in older adults)

Imaging shows T1 hypointense, heterogeneously T2 hyperintense lobulated lesion with internal cystic components and no/only minimal gadolinium enhancement (characteristic low-vascular, slow-growing tumor)
Punctate susceptibility foci/tiny hyperdense CT foci consistent with small prior hemorrhages can be seen in subependymoma
MR spectroscopy with decreased NAA, normal choline, and increased myoinositol supports a low-grade glial lesion rather than a hypercellular/neoplastic one with elevated choline

PMC11380166.diagnosis: Postoperative left common peroneal neuropathy (acute foot drop) on a background of severe chronic diabetic axonal polyneuropathy
rationale_summary: Acute onset 12 hours after aortobifemoral bypass of 0/5 left ankle dorsiflexion and eversion with numbness over the dorsum of the left foot (distribution consistent with common peroneal nerve)
Limb remained warm with preserved biphasic distal pulses, arguing against acute limb ischemia as the cause of weakness
Spine MRI showed no disc herniation or cord compression to explain the deficit
Nerve conduction study demonstrated severe chronic sensory-motor axonal peripheral polyneuropathy, supporting vulnerability to perioperative focal neuropathy
Partial strength improvement over 48 hours supports a transient neuropathic injury rather than fixed central damage

PMC11671661.diagnosis: Neuropsychiatric systemic lupus erythematosus (CNS lupus with cerebral vasculitis/vasculitic infarction presenting as aseptic meningitis)
rationale_summary: Established SLE with current flare markers (ANA 1:2560, anti-dsDNA >300 IU/mL, markedly low C3/C4) coinciding with neurologic presentation

Meningism with elevated opening pressure and inflammatory CSF (WBC 485/mm³, high protein) but gram stain/cultures and extensive PCR/antigen testing negative and no response to broad antibiotics/antivirals

Focal left temporal hypodensity on CT and MRI T2 hyperintensity with mild swelling and petechial hemorrhages on SWI, with normal MRA/MRV suggesting small-vessel inflammatory/vasculitic CNS involvement rather than large-vessel occlusion or venous thrombosis
Antiphospholipid tests negative, reducing likelihood of thrombotic APS-related stroke as the primary cause

PMC10199808.diagnosis: Intestinal spirochetosis due to Brachyspira aalborgii (Crohn's disease mimic)
rationale_summary: Stool culture grew Brachyspira aalborgii in the setting of chronic rectal tenesmus, mucus/hematochezia, and ulcerative proctitis
Only transient improvement after metronidazole, with persistent/recurrent symptoms thereafter
Histopathology showed mild active inflammation with increased eosinophils and no malignancy or alternative infectious cause identified on biopsy PCR (negative for gonorrhea, CMV, HSV)

Later discontinuous rectal/cecal/ileocecal/terminal ileal ulcerations led to presumed Crohn's, but symptoms and elevated fecal calprotectin persisted despite systemic steroids, adalimumab, and ustekinumab, supporting an IBD mimic rather than true Crohn's

PMC6519488.diagnosis: Heparin-induced thrombocytopenia (HIT) with thrombosis (HITT) due to dalteparin
rationale_summary: Recent exposure to therapeutic-dose dalteparin for PE/DVT
Marked platelet fall from 468×10^9/L to 29×10^9/L over weeks, confirmed on repeat testing
Paradoxical progression to new and extensive thromboses (worsening pulmonary emboli and new aortic mural thrombi) despite anticoagulation
Peripheral smear showing platelet clumping and fibrin stranding consistent with a consumptive/prothrombotic process rather than spurious thrombocytopenia

PMC9327270.diagnosis: Cerebral folate transport deficiency due to FOLR1 (folate receptor-α) mutation
rationale_summary: Infantile-onset epileptic encephalopathy with developmental regression, hypotonia, poor feeding and reduced social interaction beginning at ~3 months
Brain MRI shows diffuse hypomyelination/delayed myelination (T2 white-matter hyperintensity, absent PLIC myelination) with thinned corpus callosum
Extensive metabolic screening and routine labs (including ammonia/lactate) are normal, supporting a genetic hypomyelinating leukoencephalopathy
Consanguineous parents support an autosomal-recessive etiology such as FOLR1-related cerebral folate transport deficiency

PMC5237164.diagnosis: Paradoxical Toxoplasma gondii-associated immune reconstitution inflammatory syndrome (toxoplasmosis IRIS) with cerebral and spinal cord involvement (toxoplasmic myelitis)
rationale_summary: Advanced HIV with initial presentation consistent with cerebral toxoplasmosis (positive T. gondii serology; multiple ring-enhancing brain lesions with edema) and initial clinical/radiologic improvement on anti-toxoplasma therapy

After HAART initiation, new/worsening ring-enhancing brain lesions and new dorsal spinal cord T2 hyperintense lesions (T3-T9) developed with a T9 sensory level, despite reported adherence to ongoing toxoplasmosis therapy
Timing and immune recovery support IRIS (3 months after HAART; CD4 rose from 20 to 128 cells/μL; HIV RNA fell from 301,000 to 121 copies/mL)

Extensive CSF testing for other viral, bacterial, fungal, and mycobacterial causes was negative; EBV PCR positivity lacked supportive findings for CNS lymphoma (normal CSF lymphocyte phenotype; PET without hypermetabolic cerebral/medullary lesions)

PMC9022340.diagnosis: Metastatic hCG-secreting gastric choriocarcinoma causing paraneoplastic thyrotoxicosis
rationale_summary: Biochemical thyrotoxicosis (elevated total/free T4 with suppressed TSH) with diffuse increased technetium thyroid uptake but negative TSH receptor antibodies/TSI suggests non-autoimmune thyroid stimulation
18F-FDG PET-CT shows FDG-avid gastric antrum focus with extensive FDG-avid liver masses, mesenteric lymphadenopathy, and pulmonary infiltrates consistent with metastatic gastric malignancy
Systemic findings (profound anemia, markedly elevated LDH, hepatomegaly/right flank pain, elevated alkaline phosphatase) support an aggressive metastatic tumor compatible with choriocarcinoma

PMC6421145.diagnosis: Midventricular obstructive hypertrophic cardiomyopathy with apical aneurysm and left ventricular thrombus
rationale_summary: Transthoracic echocardiography showed asymmetric left ventricular hypertrophy with midventricular obstruction (peak velocities 2.00 m/s systolic and 3.67 m/s diastolic) and paradoxical early-diastolic jet flow from apex to base

Coronary CT showed no significant stenosis, supporting a nonischemic cause of the apical aneurysm and thrombus
Frequent ventricular ectopy/nonsustained ventricular tachycardia and lateral ST elevation/T-wave inversion are compatible with hypertrophic cardiomyopathy with apical aneurysm

PMC8633751.diagnosis: Primary left atrial leiomyosarcoma with subsequent vertebral metastases
rationale_summary: Initial left atrial lesion was a large, sessile, sheet-like mass densely adherent to the atrial wall and extending into the left atrial appendage/pulmonary vein orifices, which is compatible with an infiltrative cardiac neoplasm

Sixteen months later she developed aggressive lytic vertebral lesions with paravertebral soft-tissue mass; biopsy showed malignant spindle cell tumor with smooth muscle differentiation (desmin and smooth muscle actin positive), consistent with leiomyosarcoma
Extensive evaluation (mammography, Papanicolaou testing, CT chest/abdomen/pelvis) did not identify an extracardiac primary, supporting the earlier left atrial mass as the primary source

PMC7654600.diagnosis: Idiopathic granulomatous lobular mastitis (idiopathic granulomatous mastitis)

PMC9127846.diagnosis: Tuberculous arthritis/osteomyelitis of the right wrist (ostearticular tuberculosis)
rationale_summary: Chronic (>3 months) progressive wrist pain with minimal systemic symptoms (afebrile, only mild leukocytosis) despite NSAIDs and presumed osteoarthritis treatment
Imaging shows diffuse carpal destruction and osteopenia with scattered cystic bony changes, consistent with chronic granulomatous infection
MRI demonstrates a subcutaneous fluid collection communicating with the radiocarpal joint (suggesting a draining abscess/sinus tract)
Operative findings of extensive purulence, synovitis, severe bony erosion, and cartilage loss after a prolonged course are compatible with tuberculous septic arthritis

Epidemiologic risk: born in Afghanistan (higher TB prevalence) with prior international movement; HIV negative and chest radiograph without pulmonary disease (compatible with isolated extrapulmonary TB)

PMC2396655.diagnosis: Metastatic undifferentiated (immunonegative) melanoma involving the stomach/pancreas
rationale_summary: Prior high-risk nodular cutaneous melanoma (Breslow 8.3 mm, Clark IV) with subsequent widespread metastatic disease (liver, lung nodules, subcutaneous masses, bone metastases).
New confluent upper abdominal mass inseparable from gastric corpus/pancreas and endoscopic 3 cm gastro-fundal tumor consistent with visceral metastasis pattern seen in advanced melanoma.

Biopsy shows undifferentiated neoplasm and immunohistochemistry excludes carcinoma (CK−), lymphoma (LCA−), and GIST (CD34−/c-Kit−); melanoma markers are negative, consistent with a dedifferentiated/marker-negative metastatic melanoma in this clinical context.
Only weak focal vimentin positivity supports an undifferentiated/sarcomatoid phenotype rather than a specific alternative lineage.

PMC11843684.diagnosis: Transient left brachial plexus motor block from unintended spread/deep placement of local anesthetic during superficial cervical plexus block
rationale_summary: New painless unilateral (left) upper-limb weakness noted shortly after surgery in recovery
Weakness involved multiple myotomes (shoulder, elbow, wrist/finger abduction) consistent with brachial plexus involvement rather than a single peripheral nerve
A bilateral superficial cervical plexus block with ropivacaine was performed using landmark technique, which can inadvertently spread to/deposit near the interscalene/brachial plexus region

PMC4862300.diagnosis: Warburg micro syndrome (autosomal recessive RAB3GAP-related neuro-ocular syndrome)
rationale_summary: Consanguineous parents with severe global developmental delay, microcephaly, and marked growth failure
Congenital bilateral cataracts with microphthalmia/microcornea and atonic nonreactive pupils; abnormal VEP/BAER
Male hypogonadism features: bilateral undescended testes and micropenis
Neuroimaging abnormalities typical of the syndrome: corpus callosum hypoplasia and cortical malformation (pachygyria) with ventriculomegaly
Upper-limb hypotonia with lower-limb hypertonia/brisk reflexes consistent with spasticity described in Warburg micro syndrome

Myeloproliferative neoplasms excluded: normal karyotype, FISH/PCR negative for BCR-ABL (no t(9;22)), JAK2 V617F negative

PMC9069601.diagnosis: Chronic Q fever (Coxiella burnetii) vascular graft infection causing immune-complex leukocytoclastic vasculitis and glomerulonephritis (lupus-like nephritis mimic)
rationale_summary: Two-year history of subjective fevers/night sweats with markedly elevated ESR/CRP and high rheumatoid factor

Palpable purpura with skin biopsy showing leukocytoclastic vasculitis with IgM/C3 deposition and urinary acanthocytes/hematuria indicating immune-complex small-vessel vasculitis with glomerular involvement

Renal biopsy with mesangial hypercellularity and mesangial electron-dense deposits with C3/C1q/IgM staining, consistent with immune-complex GN but serologies atypical for lupus (only weak ANA, dsDNA negative, complements normal)

Relapsing, steroid-responsive inflammatory syndrome and repeatedly negative routine blood cultures/standard infectious workup, plus history of an indwelling femoral crossover graft, supports chronic culture-negative infection such as Q fever involving prosthetic material

PMC8962241.diagnosis: Cerebral arterial air embolism secondary to lung cancer invading the left atrium (bronchopulmonary venous/left atrial fistula)
rationale_summary: Acute focal neurologic deficits (aphasia, left-sided weakness, neglect) on day 2
Head CT showed multiple small low-density areas interpreted as intracranial air emboli
Brain MRI had cortical/subcortical diffusion abnormalities consistent with acute ischemic injury from embolic events
Chest CT and echocardiography showed the left hilar tumor directly invading the left atrium and left main bronchus, providing a route for air to enter the systemic circulation

PMC3608321.diagnosis: Occult ectopic ACTH syndrome (ACTH-dependent Cushing syndrome from a nonpituitary ACTH-secreting tumor)
rationale_summary: Marked hypercortisolism (UFC 1300 nmol/24 h; serum cortisol 600-1000 nmol/L without diurnal variation) with detectable ACTH (30-45 ng/L) indicates ACTH-dependent Cushing syndrome
No suppression with 1-mg or 8-mg dexamethasone and no ACTH rise on CRH stimulation argue against pituitary Cushing disease
Pituitary MRI negative and inferior petrosal sinus sampling shows no central-to-peripheral ACTH gradient, supporting an ectopic (nonpituitary) ACTH source
Cross-sectional imaging and 111In-pentetreotide scan did not localize a tumor, consistent with an occult ectopic ACTH source

PMC8186929.diagnosis: Anomalous origin of the right coronary artery from the pulmonary artery (ARCAPA) with coronary steal
rationale_summary: Coronary angiography showed markedly ectatic left main/LAD/LCx without obstructive stenoses and inability to engage the RCA ostium
Left coronary contrast injection demonstrated retrograde filling of the RCA via extensive collaterals
The RCA was felt to empty into the pulmonary artery/right ventricular outflow tract, consistent with anomalous RCA origin/connection to the pulmonary artery

Presentation with acute biventricular systolic dysfunction, shock, and large troponin rise despite no flow-limiting coronary disease supports ischemia from coronary steal rather than atherosclerotic ACS

PMC10191493.diagnosis: Isolated gastroduodenal Crohn disease causing a duodenal stricture (gastric outlet obstruction)
rationale_summary: 12-year-old with weight loss, decreased appetite, abdominal pain, and bilious vomiting with epigastric fullness
Imaging (UGI series, MRE) showed marked gastric and proximal duodenal distention with decompression distally, consistent with an obstructing proximal duodenal stricture
Endoscopy demonstrated complete obstruction in the second portion of the duodenum
Labs showed thrombocytosis and elevated inflammatory markers supporting an inflammatory etiology
Biopsies showed chronic gastritis and active duodenitis with no granulomas, viral inclusions, or malignancy, fitting inflammatory bowel disease despite nonspecific histology

PMC2934912.diagnosis: Acute brucellosis (Brucella infection) presenting with cholestatic hepatitis and SIADH

PMC5807253.diagnosis: Metformin-associated lactic acidosis (MALA) precipitated by acute kidney injury from volume depletion/shock
rationale_summary: On metformin 1000 mg twice daily
Profound high-anion gap metabolic acidosis (pH 6.57, HCO3 2, anion gap 30) with marked hyperlactatemia (lactate 16.3 mmol/L)
Severe acute kidney injury (creatinine 8.07 mg/dL from baseline 0.86) after 3 days of vomiting/diarrhea and hypotension, favoring metformin accumulation
Shock with hypotension requiring vasopressors and PEA arrest, consistent with severe lactic acidosis physiology

PMC5896219.diagnosis: Acute right forearm/hand compartment syndrome secondary to acute gout flare with tophaceous flexor tenosynovitis
rationale_summary: History of crystal-proven gout with progressive forearm/hand pain and swelling unrelieved by NSAIDs
Exam showed classic compartment syndrome findings (tense volar compartments, pain with passive stretch, sensory changes, decreased capillary refill)
Measured elevated compartment pressures (20-24 mm Hg volar forearm; 45-48 mm Hg carpal tunnel/palm) prompting urgent fasciotomies
Joint aspiration demonstrated monosodium urate crystals with very high synovial WBC (91,650/mm3)
Intraoperative finding of small tophi in flexor tendon synovial sheaths with no pus, odor, or necrosis supports gout-related etiology rather than infection

PMC4100269.diagnosis: Lipoprotein X-mediated severe hypercholesterolemia secondary to obstructive jaundice from common bile duct obstruction
rationale_summary: Marked cholestasis with imaging/ERCP showing abrupt mid-CBD occlusion and a long tight proximal CBD stricture with biliary dilatation

PMC6342725.diagnosis: Flecainide-induced acute drug pneumonitis (drug-induced interstitial lung disease/hypersensitivity reaction)
rationale_summary: Symptoms (fever, chills, dry cough, rapidly worsening hypoxemia) began ~5 hours after first flecainide dose and recurred/worsened ~3 hours after the second dose
Imaging showed diffuse bilateral ground-glass opacities with multifocal perilobular consolidation and mild traction bronchiectasis/bronchiolectasis, consistent with acute inflammatory lung injury
Leukocytosis accompanied febrile episodes
Findings argued against cardiogenic pulmonary edema (LVEF 65-70%, small/collapsed IVC, BNP 186 pg/mL, no mitral stenosis)
Clinical deterioration temporally linked to flecainide exposure and prompted drug discontinuation

PMC10310912.diagnosis: False-positive intraoperative cholangiogram "contrast leak" due to transient intraluminal duodenal contrast (no true duodenal or biliary perforation)

PMC10971827.diagnosis: Macrophage activation syndrome (secondary hemophagocytic lymphohistiocytosis) complicating systemic lupus erythematosus
rationale_summary: Meets 2018 ACR/EULAR criteria for systemic lupus erythematosus (high-titer ANA, very high anti-dsDNA, low C3/C4, cytopenias, proteinuria).
Persistent fever with progressive pancytopenia, markedly elevated ferritin, hypertriglyceridemia, and falling fibrinogen (to 94 mg/dL).
HScore 195 (80-88% probability of MAS/HLH) with negative cultures/procalcitonin and poor response to broad-spectrum antibiotics.
Bone marrow showed reactive changes with cells containing eosinophilic amorphous nuclear material, supporting an autoimmune flare context.

PMC8793420.diagnosis: Primary cardiac lymphoma (likely diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma) presenting as a right atrial mass

PMC11491757.diagnosis: Spontaneous cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) rhinorrhea due to a skull base leak (likely related to idiopathic intracranial hypertension)
rationale_summary: Copious persistent clear rhinorrhea (left-predominant) that worsens with bending/lifting and on waking suggests a CSF leak rather than rhinitis
Prior chronic severe frontal/bitemporal headache with tinnitus and visual disturbances that resolved when clear rhinorrhea began is consistent with high intracranial pressure relieved by CSF drainage
CT showed an isolated air-fluid level in the left sphenoid sinus without sinonasal mass or obstruction, supporting CSF tracking into the sphenoid sinus
Obesity and the described pressure-type symptoms raise concern for idiopathic intracranial hypertension as an underlying driver of spontaneous CSF leak

PMC2329657.diagnosis: Severe iron-deficiency anemia with extreme reactive thrombocytosis
rationale_summary: Profound microcytic anemia (Hb 4.6 g/dl, MCV 58 fl) with very low ferritin (<1.5 ng/ml), serum iron (0.4 μmol/l), and transferrin saturation (0.4%)
Bone marrow showed depleted iron stores and increased non-dysplastic erythropoiesis, supporting iron deficiency rather than a primary marrow neoplasm
Marked thrombocytosis (1703 ×10^3/μl) resolved to normal after transfusions and iron supplementation without cytoreductive therapy
Normal inflammatory markers (CRP/ESR) and normal cytogenetics argue against other secondary causes or a clonal myeloproliferative disorder

rationale_summary: Subacute (1 month) severe radicular back/leg pain followed by progressive bilateral proximal leg weakness, sensory loss, and autonomic dysfunction (urinary retention, constipation) consistent with meningoradiculitis
MRI shows bilateral cauda equina nerve root enhancement (L1-L5), supporting inflammatory radiculitis
CSF has lymphocytic pleocytosis (114 cells/mm3) with elevated protein (149 mg/dL), fitting infectious/inflammatory meningoradiculitis rather than classic GBS
Possible associated cranial neuropathy suggested by bilateral CN VII enhancement on brain MRI
Occurred in summer with preceding self-resolving erythematous rashes, compatible with Lyme exposure despite no recognized tick bite

PMC8050037.diagnosis: Cystic dilatation of gallbladder subserosal glands (glands of Luschka)
rationale_summary: Imaging and gross exam showed multiple small (3-5 mm) cysts confined to the gallbladder wall on the serosal (liver) side, containing clear serous fluid and not filling with contrast (no communication with lumen).
Histology showed subserosal cysts lined by bland cuboidal to low columnar epithelium with adjacent small tubular gland-like structures, without Rokitansky-Aschoff sinuses or mucosal invagination.
Cysts were surrounded by dense fibrous tissue lacking the usual gallbladder fibromuscular layer, supporting origin from subserosal glands rather than adenomyomatosis or mucosal diverticula.

PMC6475540.diagnosis: Malignant granular cell tumor of the anterior abdominal wall (soft tissue)
rationale_summary: Slowly enlarging firm subcutaneous abdominal wall mass, confined to abdominal wall on CT
Histology shows nests of large cells with abundant granular eosinophilic cytoplasm and eosinophilic globules separated by fibrous bands
PAS highlights cytoplasmic granules/globules; strong S100 positivity supports Schwannian granular cell tumor phenotype, with CD68 and vimentin co-positivity
Malignant features present: pleomorphic vesicular nuclei, focal necrosis, and increased mitotic activity (4/10 HPF) in a relatively large (7 cm) deep lesion
Negative cytokeratin, SMA, desmin, and myogenin argues against carcinoma and myogenic/smooth muscle tumors

PMC8318494.diagnosis: Congenital unilateral absence of the right pulmonary artery (UAPA) causing severe pulmonary arterial hypertension and right heart failure
rationale_summary: Progressive dyspnea, JVD, edema, loud P2 with echo showing RV enlargement/dysfunction consistent with pulmonary hypertension/right heart failure
Perfusion scan shows absent right-lung perfusion with normal left-lung perfusion
Pulmonary angiography and 4D CT show complete absence of the right pulmonary artery from the main pulmonary trunk

Systemic collateral supply to the underdeveloped right lung from the right lateral thoracic and inferior phrenic arteries supports congenital UAPA rather than chronic thromboembolism (no pouch defects/intimal irregularities)
Right heart catheterization demonstrates severe precapillary pulmonary hypertension (mPAP 74 mm Hg, PAWP 12 mm Hg, PVR 35.4 WU)

Associated eosinophilia and elevated IgE, which can accompany Hodgkin lymphoma

SVC syndrome physiology: dilated anterior chest wall veins plus CT venogram showing thrombus in right internal jugular, subclavian, brachiocephalic veins and SVC with extensive collateral channels and nodal compression of the right internal jugular vein
Pulmonary/serosal involvement compatible with thoracic malignancy/lymphoma (right middle lobe collapse/consolidation, small pleural effusion, mild pericardial effusion)
Broad infectious (including TB) and autoimmune/hypercoagulable evaluations were negative; FNAC was non-diagnostic, which is common in lymphoma and warrants tissue biopsy

PMC11460033.diagnosis: Central diabetes insipidus associated with primary partial empty sella syndrome
rationale_summary: Marked polydipsia/polyuria with dilute urine (specific gravity ~1.005; urine osmolality 150 mOsm/kg)
Water deprivation increased serum osmolality (298 mOsm/kg) without appropriate urine concentration (urine osmolality 210 mOsm/kg), supporting diabetes insipidus
Urine concentrated appropriately after desmopressin (specific gravity 1.020; urine osmolality 600 mOsm/kg), indicating central (AVP-deficient) DI
Brain MRI showed partial empty sella with a flattened pituitary and no other intracranial pathology; anterior pituitary hormones were normal

PMC6252232.diagnosis: Myelodysplastic syndrome (refractory anemia) with complete hematologic remission temporally associated with intravesical Bacillus Calmette-Guérin therapy for non-muscle-invasive bladder cancer
rationale_summary: Longstanding macrocytic anemia with low reticulocytes and later thrombocytopenia, with bone marrow hypercellularity and dysplasia and blasts <5% (refractory anemia-type MDS)
Became transfusion-dependent for RBCs (and intermittently platelets) for years
After superficial low-grade papillary urothelial carcinoma was resected, transfusion needs decreased but persisted

PMC4082171.diagnosis: Synthetic cannabinoid (K2/Spice) intoxication causing multiorgan toxicity (seizure, acute kidney injury, cardiomyopathy with pulmonary edema/ARDS)
rationale_summary: Young man with tonic-clonic seizure followed by severe agitation/confusion

Routine urine toxicology negative for stimulants/opioids/PCP/cocaine and only positive for cannabinoids (plus benzodiazepines given in ED), suggesting an undetected designer drug rather than typical agents
Rapidly progressive acute kidney injury (creatinine 1.4→4.3 mg/dL) with GI symptoms (abdominal pain, vomiting, diarrhea)

Acute hypoxic respiratory failure with bilateral alveolar infiltrates requiring high PEEP and echocardiographic global LV dysfunction (EF 30%), consistent with toxic cardiomyopathy/volume overload contributing to pulmonary edema/ARDS
Clinical improvement after hemodialysis with recovery of oxygenation, urine output, and creatinine supports a toxin-associated AKI with secondary cardiopulmonary decompensation

PMC10688243.diagnosis: Primary endometrial adenocarcinoma with intestinal (colorectal-type) differentiation (high-grade mucinous/intestinal-type endometrial carcinoma)
rationale_summary: Polypoid 2 cm lesion arising in the uterine wall/endometrium in a postmenopausal woman with abnormal bleeding

PMC10888719.diagnosis: Neuromyelitis optica spectrum disorder (NMOSD) presenting with longitudinally extensive transverse myelitis and optic neuritis after Pfizer-BioNTech COVID-19 booster

PMC6099017.diagnosis: Primary hepatic T-cell-rich B‑cell lymphoma (T‑cell-rich large B‑cell lymphoma variant)
rationale_summary: Solitary, well-vascularized hepatic mass with cytology most consistent with lymphoma (lymphoglandular background; predominance of intermediate lymphocytes)
Flow cytometry shows a predominant CD3+ T-cell population with only a small CD21+ B-cell population, supporting a T-cell-rich pattern rather than purely reactive inflammation
PARR demonstrates a reproducible clonal immunoglobulin heavy-chain rearrangement with polyclonal TCR rearrangements, indicating an underlying clonal B-cell neoplasm admixed with reactive T cells
No thoracic radiographic evidence of disseminated disease, supporting a primary hepatic presentation

PMC3130996.diagnosis: Herpes simplex virus type 2-associated lumbosacral (S1-S3) radiculopathy with postherpetic neuralgia
rationale_summary: Acute bilateral vulval blisters diagnosed as genital herpes with rapid resolution on valacyclovir and later milder recurrence of blisters
Persistent burning pain and marked hyperaesthesia in a dermatomal distribution (S1-S3), without motor deficit, consistent with radicular neuropathic pain after HSV reactivation
Recurrence of identical hyperaesthesia after recurrent vulval blisters supports HSV-associated neuralgia rather than a primary noninfectious neuropathy
PCR from healed sites was negative for HSV/VZV, which can occur after lesion resolution; no clinical history of shingles and VZV PCR was negative
Complete symptom resolution with amitriptyline supports neuropathic pain (postherpetic neuralgia-like syndrome)

PMC8414607.diagnosis: Delayed post-hypoxic leukoencephalopathy (DPHL) following hypoxic respiratory failure from suspected opioid/benzodiazepine overdose
rationale_summary: Developed subacute neuropsychiatric deterioration to akinetic mutism in the weeks after an episode of acute respiratory failure requiring intubation/ICU care in the setting of suspected opioid/benzodiazepine overdose
Brain MRI repeatedly showed extensive, diffuse nonenhancing cerebral white-matter T2 hyperintensities with bilateral basal ganglia signal changes, without restricted diffusion or enhancement

PMC7501889.diagnosis: Arrhythmogenic right ventricular cardiomyopathy (ARVC) causing isolated right ventricular failure with severe functional tricuspid regurgitation
rationale_summary: Young patient with syncope and documented non-sustained ventricular tachycardia
ECG shows right bundle branch block and atrial fibrillation in the setting of marked right-sided chamber enlargement
Echocardiography shows severe right ventricular dilatation with severe tricuspid regurgitation while left ventricular size/function is normal
Right heart catheterization shows normal pulmonary arterial pressures and pulmonary vascular resistance, arguing against pulmonary hypertension as the cause of RV failure

Repeat echocardiogram showed new severe global LV systolic dysfunction (LVEF 25% with global hypokinesis) consistent with myocarditis rather than focal ischemia
Hemodynamics and imaging led clinicians to favor cardiogenic shock over ongoing septic shock

I'm weighing other possibilities but favor autoimmune myocarditis after infection clearance.

PMC4109228.diagnosis: Calcium pyrophosphate deposition disease (CPPD) presenting as pseudo-rheumatoid inflammatory polyarthritis with rapidly destructive hip arthropathy (tophaceous/tumoral pseudogout)
rationale_summary: Progressive inflammatory polyarthritis with prolonged morning stiffness, synovitis in MTPs/knee, erosive foot changes and periarticular osteopenia but negative RF and anti-CCP (pseudo-RA pattern)
Right hip aspirates repeatedly showed CPPD crystals; CT also demonstrated a periarticular calcified soft-tissue mass consistent with tumoral/tophi-like CPPD deposition

Imaging showed very rapid right hip joint destruction over 5 months (complete femoral head loss, acetabular erosion, subluxation) with marked synovial hypertrophy on MRI, fitting destructive crystal arthropathy
Extensive negative blood cultures and malignancy work-up argue against septic or neoplastic causes of the destructive arthropathy

PMC10810688.diagnosis: X-linked hypophosphatemia (FGF23-mediated renal phosphate wasting causing hypophosphatemic osteomalacia with enthesopathy)
rationale_summary: Longstanding enthesopathy/ligament calcification and sacroiliac fusion with poor response to NSAIDs and adalimumab and HLA-B27 negative, arguing against inflammatory ankylosing spondylitis
Hypophosphatemia (phosphate 1.9 mg/dL) with elevated alkaline phosphatase and secondary hyperparathyroidism (PTH 115 pg/mL) consistent with osteomalacia
24-hour urine phosphate 1300 mg is inappropriately normal for hypophosphatemia, suggesting renal phosphate wasting (FGF23-mediated)
Skeletal/dental features (femoral bowing, subtrochanteric insufficiency fractures, chronic dental abscesses/fractures/extractions) and early hearing loss support lifelong XLH phenotype

PMC4010020.diagnosis: Late-onset urea cycle disorder (most consistent with ornithine transcarbamylase deficiency) causing noncirrhotic hyperammonemic encephalopathy
rationale_summary: Marked hyperammonemia (202→516 µmol/L) with coma and EEG triphasic waves
No cirrhosis or significant hepatic synthetic dysfunction (near-normal AST/ALT, bilirubin, INR; CT only fatty liver)
Ammonia elevation persisted despite aggressive lactulose and required dialysis, suggesting impaired ammonia detoxification rather than typical hepatic encephalopathy
Recent catabolic stress/exposure (fatigue over 2 weeks; recent methylprednisolone taper) can precipitate decompensation in partial urea-cycle defects

PMC11359919.diagnosis: Neuroretinitis (most consistent with Bartonella henselae-associated neuroretinitis)
rationale_summary: Acute, painless unilateral central visual loss with relative afferent pupillary defect and centrocecal scotoma
Fundus/OCT/FFA: diffuse optic disc swelling with leakage and optic nerve-to-macula intraretinal/subretinal fluid

PMC7869131.diagnosis: Williams-Campbell syndrome (congenital bronchomalacia due to deficiency of bronchial cartilage)
rationale_summary: Lifelong history of recurrent pneumonias/bronchiectasis beginning in childhood with multiple unusual respiratory pathogens (e.g., Nocardia, Mycobacterium avium-intracellulare).
CT shows ballooning cystic bronchiectasis in distal (4th-6th order) bronchi with marked collapse on expiratory imaging, consistent with bronchomalacia from absent/deficient bronchial cartilage.
Workup for common causes of bronchiectasis is unrevealing (normal immunoglobulins, normal sweat chloride, normal α-1 antitrypsin; negative autoimmune and Aspergillus testing).
Pulmonary function demonstrates fixed obstructive disease without bronchodilator reversibility, compatible with structural airway abnormality.

PMC4857264.diagnosis: Autoimmune myelofibrosis secondary to systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE)
rationale_summary: Transfusion-dependent severe anemia with bone marrow showing hypercellularity/panhyperplasia and moderate reticulin fibrosis but lacking typical primary myelofibrosis features (no megakaryocyte atypia/dysplasia; no leukoerythroblastosis)
Myeloproliferative driver mutations negative (JAK2 V617F/exon 12 and MPL)
Systemic autoimmune context (family history of SLE, photosensitivity, arthralgias)
Anemia showed spontaneous fluctuation and steroid responsiveness (dexamethasone led to temporary resolution), supporting an autoimmune marrow process rather than clonal PMF

PMC10943559.diagnosis: Peri-Hisian microreentrant atrial tachycardia (adenosine-sensitive focal AT)
rationale_summary: Regular narrow-QRS long-RP SVT with earliest atrial activation at the His-bundle electrogram
Adenosine 5′-triphosphate terminated the tachycardia without atrioventricular block, supporting an atrial (non-AV nodal dependent) mechanism
Ventricular burst pacing during tachycardia showed ventriculoatrial dissociation, excluding AV reentry via an accessory pathway
Differential atrial pacing showed no VA linking and a postpacing VA interval difference of 22 ms (>14 ms), favoring atrial tachycardia over AVNRT
Entrainment findings indicated a slow-conduction zone with an entrance lateral to the earliest activation site, consistent with microreentrant AT near the His region

PMC8780476.diagnosis: COVID-19-associated cardioinhibitory reflex (vasovagal) syncope due to autonomic dysfunction
rationale_summary: Reproducible syncope triggered by moving from supine to sitting with prodrome (yawning, dizziness, visual blurring) and progressive sinus bradycardia over minutes despite initially preserved blood pressure

ECG during events showed marked sinus bradycardia without PR prolongation or AV block, and Holter captured sinus pauses up to 3.4 s with escape rhythms (idioventricular rhythm/slow VT) consistent with a cardioinhibitory mechanism

MRI shows multiple periventricular/posterior subcortical cystic lesions with surrounding T2 hyperintensity and no contrast enhancement, supporting late radiation injury rather than enhancing tumor recurrence or active infection

PMC6521521.diagnosis: Moyamoya disease (chronic cerebral hypoperfusion with watershed ischemic lesions)
rationale_summary: Recurrent transient neurologic deficits (right facial/arm numbness; intermittent left-arm weakness over years)
MRI shows multiple subcortical and periventricular T2/FLAIR hyperintense lesions predominantly in the ACA-MCA watershed zone
Lesions lack restricted diffusion, supporting non-acute/chronic ischemic injury rather than acute infarct
CSF and inflammatory/autoimmune laboratory studies are unremarkable, arguing against inflammatory demyelination
Mild linear cortical enhancement can be seen with pial collateralization in moyamoya, prompting recommendation for brain MRA to evaluate cerebrovascular etiology

No alternative source found despite presumed ischemic bowel (exploratory laparotomy negative; CXR/ECG/toxicology unremarkable)

PMC3080319.diagnosis: Chronic sclerosing sialadenitis (Küttner tumor), an IgG4-related submandibular gland disease mimicking malignancy
rationale_summary: Painless, elastic-hard enlargement of the submandibular gland with reduced salivary flow suggests chronic inflammatory/sclerosing sialadenitis rather than a primary epithelial tumor
Markedly suspicious imaging (enhancing mass with apparent invasion and lymphadenopathy) but PET uptake confined to the submandibular region supports a localized inflammatory pseudotumor-like process

PMC6581986.diagnosis: Septo-optic dysplasia (de Morsier syndrome) spectrum with achiasma
rationale_summary: MRI showed bilateral optic nerve hypoplasia with absence of the optic chiasm (achiasma).

Endocrine evaluation demonstrated pituitary hormone deficiencies (growth hormone deficiency and hypothyroidism), and MRI showed flattened pituitary tissue associated with a transsphenoidal basal cephalocele.

PMC10302296.diagnosis: Right cranial mediastinal (thoracic) abscess with septic pleural effusion causing extrinsic right-heart compression (pseudo-right-sided heart failure)
rationale_summary: Thoracic ultrasound showed a large (30×25 cm) echogenic right-sided mass severely compressing the right heart with adjacent pleural fluid; pericardium/valves appeared normal (argues against pericarditis).

Clinical signs of impaired venous return/right-sided congestion (bilateral jugular distension with positive compression test, dewlap edema) and attenuated right cardiac/pulmonary sounds are consistent with intrathoracic compression/effusion.
Pleural fluid was a cloudy orange-brown septic exudate, supporting an infectious intrathoracic process such as an abscess/empyema.
Systemic inflammatory changes (neutrophilia, lymphopenia, hyperproteinemia with hypoalbuminemia) fit a chronic/septic inflammatory condition.

PMC7883144.diagnosis: Nitrofurantoin-induced acute hypersensitivity reaction presenting as systemic inflammatory response syndrome (sepsis mimic)
rationale_summary: Abrupt fever, chills, pleuritic symptoms, marked neutrophilic leukocytosis, and hypotension developed within <24 hours of starting nitrofurantoin
Reproducible recurrence with re-challenge two months later within the same time frame
Rapid symptom resolution within ~24 hours after stopping nitrofurantoin, inconsistent with true pyelonephritis/sepsis course
Initial episode had low lactate and only mild urinalysis abnormalities, supporting a noninfectious drug reaction rather than severe bacterial infection

PMC4632472.diagnosis: Hyperfunctioning struma ovarii (thyroid tissue in an ovarian teratoma) causing persistent thyrotoxicosis after total thyroidectomy
rationale_summary: Recurrent thyrotoxicosis 2 weeks after total thyroidectomy with elevated free T4 and suppressed TSH

PMC11783208.diagnosis: Torsion of a pedunculated subserosal uterine leiomyoma with ischemic (hemorrhagic) infarction
rationale_summary: MRI showed a solid mass arising from the right uterine cornua via a broad pedicle with a bridging-vessel sign, supporting uterine leiomyoma origin
The pedicle was thickened and twisted in a spiral configuration, consistent with torsion
Most of the mass was nonenhancing with only a thin enhancing rim/small enhancing area near attachment, suggesting ischemia/infarction from torsion
Right ovary enlargement with stromal hemorrhagic signal and ipsilateral ureterohydronephrosis from ureter kinking support a torsion-related pelvic process

PMC5918180.diagnosis: Antidromic atrioventricular reentrant tachycardia using a decremental right atriofascicular (Mahaim) accessory pathway
rationale_summary: Clinical WCT had LBBB morphology with left superior axis and terminated with adenosine

Sinus ECG showed no baseline preexcitation, yet atrial pacing with a switch to AV nodal slow-pathway conduction produced fusion then fully preexcited QRS complexes with the His signal displaced into the ventricular electrogram
Incremental atrial pacing caused progressive prolongation of the stimulus-delta interval, consistent with a decremental accessory pathway typical of an atriofascicular (Mahaim) pathway
Induced WCT had identical QRS morphology and terminated with adenosine or rapid ventricular pacing, supporting AV reentry with AV node participation
Fusion beats during induction and during tachycardia and concentric retrograde conduction with adenosine-induced VA block support reentrant tachycardia with retrograde limb through the AV node

PMC5368394.diagnosis: Disseminated Mycobacterium marinum infection (fish-tank granuloma with sporotrichoid spread)

Development of firm nodules along extensor surfaces with proximal spread (sporotrichoid pattern) and later lower-extremity nodules
Repeated aspirations/cultures negative on routine bacterial studies; autoimmune serologies negative and inflammatory markers normal despite presumed RA
Marked worsening after escalation of immunosuppression, especially TNF-α inhibitors (adalimumab/etanercept), consistent with occult atypical mycobacterial infection

PMC4387347.diagnosis: Pituitary hyperplasia secondary to severe primary hypothyroidism from autoimmune thyroiditis (Hashimoto thyroiditis)

Autoimmune thyroiditis supported by elevated anti-thyroid peroxidase antibodies and atrophic hypoechoic thyroid on ultrasound

Sellar/suprasellar enlarging lesion with stalk/chiasm elevation and mild hyperprolactinemia consistent with pituitary hyperplasia from excess TRH/TSH drive rather than a TSH-secreting adenoma (which would raise T3/T4)

PMC8922707.diagnosis: Fulminant giant cell myocarditis (with associated pericarditis)
rationale_summary: Previously healthy 44-year-old with viral-like prodrome followed by marked troponin elevation and inflammatory markers, small pericardial effusion, and no obstructive coronary disease on CT
Rapid deterioration over days from preserved LV function to LVEF 35% with pulmonary congestion and low cardiac output (CI 1.65 L/min/m^2; PCWP 34 mmHg; LVEDP 29 mmHg)
Progression of conduction disease from first-degree AV block to complete heart block with syncope requiring temporary pacing

Cardiac MRI showing myocardial edema with multifocal subepicardial and subendocardial late gadolinium enhancement plus pericardial inflammation, consistent with aggressive myocarditis pattern typical of giant cell myocarditis
Extensive negative infectious and autoimmune/vasculitis workup supporting noninfectious fulminant myocarditis requiring endomyocardial biopsy for definitive diagnosis

PMC5120475.diagnosis: ROHHAD syndrome (Rapid-onset Obesity with Hypothalamic Dysfunction, Hypoventilation, and Autonomic Dysregulation)
rationale_summary: Abrupt onset at 4 years with extreme hyperphagia and rapid weight gain (~1 kg every 10-15 days)
Multiple autonomic features: decreased lacrimation/nasal secretions/sweating, cold blue extremities, alternating diarrhea/constipation, polyuria/polydipsia, nocturnal enuresis
Neurobehavioral/sleep changes after obesity onset: anxiety/rage/aggression, fatigue, social withdrawal, prolonged sleep and daytime somnolence
Brain MRI shows pituitary gland enlargement without other structural lesions, supporting hypothalamic-pituitary involvement despite initially normal endocrine labs

PMC8065272.diagnosis: Page kidney-type renin‑mediated hypertension from extrinsic right renal compression due to extensive perihepatic abdominal packing
rationale_summary: New-onset severe, refractory and paroxysmal hypertension developed after damage-control surgery with extensive abdominal packing for right-lobe liver injury
Hypertension persisted despite adequate analgesia/sedation status (extubated, no pain) and multiple antihypertensives/diuretics; fluid balance markedly positive (+10 L)

Secondary causes investigated were unrevealing: CT angiography showed no aortic dissection, renal Doppler was normal, and CT showed no renal hematoma, supporting a nonvascular, extrinsic renal compression mechanism (Page kidney) from packing

PMC4898247.diagnosis: False-positive I-131 uptake in chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL) lymphadenopathy (axillary/inguinal nodes), not thyroid cancer metastases
rationale_summary: Known CLL with enlarged axillary and retroperitoneal lymph nodes on CT prior to ablation, attributed to CLL
Post-therapy WBS showed bilateral axillary and inguinal uptake; SPECT/CT localized uptake to enlarged axillary lymph nodes

Primary thyroid cancer was a 1.2-mm papillary thyroid microcarcinoma confined to the thyroid without extrathyroidal extension/vascular invasion, making distant nodal spread to axilla/inguinal regions unlikely
Pre-therapy WBS showed only tiny thyroid remnant uptake and no definite extra-cervical disease, supporting a non-thyroid-cancer cause for the new nodal uptake

PMC11522587.diagnosis: Hansen disease (leprosy) with chronic peripheral neuropathy causing acro-osteolysis and neuropathic arthropathy
rationale_summary: Decades-long hypopigmented "map-like" patches on hands/wrists/knees/ankles beginning ~30 years prior
Prominent peripheral neuropathy (reduced sensation up to knees, hand muscle atrophy, tingling) with motor deformity (foot inversion)
Progressive painless digital shortening with radiographic complete distal phalangeal resorption and destructive lytic/erosive foot changes consistent with neuropathic osteoarthropathy
From the Philippines (endemic area) and exam lacks typical systemic sclerosis features (no sclerodactyly, telangiectasias, or marked microstomia) despite acro-osteolysis

PMC7934766.diagnosis: Secondary (vascular) mania due to cerebral small-vessel ischemic disease (subcortical ischemic vascular cognitive impairment)
rationale_summary: New-onset manic/psychotic syndrome at age 71 (irritability, disinhibition, impulsive spending, hyperphagia, pressured/disorganized speech with flight of ideas, grandiose/paranoid delusions) without prior bipolar history
Prominent executive dysfunction with relatively preserved global cognition (FAB 9/18 with impaired inhibition/conceptualisation; MMSE 27/30)
Neurologic signs suggesting subcortical/frontal involvement (perseveration, impaired abstraction, broad-based shuffling gait)

MRI shows advanced small-vessel ischemic disease with confluent periventricular/subcortical white-matter hyperintensities and frontal-predominant atrophy, supporting a vascular etiology for late-life mood/behavioral change

PMC9759948.diagnosis: POEMS syndrome (paraneoplastic plasma cell disorder with osteosclerotic bone lesions, despite negative immunofixation)
rationale_summary: Symmetric sensorimotor demyelinating polyneuropathy on nerve conduction studies with burning paresthesias and hyporeflexia
Characteristic multisystem features: hyperpigmentation/erythroderma, sclerodermoid changes, leukonychia, digital clubbing, livedo, and marked weight loss
Marked thrombocytosis (847×10^9/L), anasarca/lumbar edema, jugular venous distention, and bilateral papilledema
Pulmonary hypertension on echocardiography
Osteosclerotic vertebral lesions (L4-S1) with abnormal enhancement of paraspinal muscles/subcutaneous tissue on MRI
Associated lymphadenopathy and endocrinopathy symptoms (polymenorrhea) supporting POEMS even with repeatedly negative serum/urine immunofixation

PMC10832606.diagnosis: Desmoid-type fibromatosis (aggressive fibromatosis) of the groin compressing the common femoral vein
rationale_summary: Progressive unilateral right leg edema with duplex excluding DVT suggests proximal venous outflow obstruction
CT/duplex/MRI identified a 3-5 cm right groin mass dorsal to and compressing the common femoral vein with focal narrowing
MRI showed a solid soft-tissue lesion (T1 isointense to muscle, mildly T2 hyperintense, slight contrast enhancement) compatible with fibrous spindle-cell tumor
Fine-needle biopsy demonstrated proliferating spindle cells without definitive malignant features, fitting desmoid-type fibromatosis

PMC3978404.diagnosis: Constrictive pericarditis causing congestive hepatopathy (cardiac cirrhosis)

No serologic evidence of viral, autoimmune, metabolic, or cholestatic liver disease and no alcohol use
Enlarged inferior vena cava and elevated right atrial pressure suggest systemic venous congestion

Progressive exertional dyspnea with mild pulmonary hypertension and elevated filling pressures on right-heart catheterization compatible with impaired diastolic filling from constriction

PMC6751912.diagnosis: Late-onset urea cycle disorder (most consistent with ornithine transcarbamylase deficiency) causing recurrent hyperammonemic encephalopathy mimicking nonconvulsive status epilepticus
rationale_summary: 5-year history of episodic prolonged unresponsiveness with post-event amnesia, previously labeled psychiatric/catatonia
EEG showed generalized ~2.5 Hz sharp/slow-wave discharges that transiently suppressed with diazepam but recurred without sustained clinical improvement despite multiple antiseizure therapies
Workup for structural, infectious, and autoimmune encephalitis was unremarkable (normal MRI, CSF, antibody panel)
Mild hyperammonemia with normal liver-function tests suggests a non-hepatic cause of hyperammonemia

Clinical deterioration with rising ammonia, cerebral edema, and near-isoelectric EEG prompted treatment with dextrose, sodium benzoate/phenylbutyrate, and hemodialysis-standard management for urea cycle-related hyperammonemia
Valproate/topiramate exposure temporally associated with worsening hyperammonemia, a known precipitant in latent urea cycle disorders (especially OTC deficiency)

PMC11116953.diagnosis: Idiopathic left posterior fascicular ventricular tachycardia (fascicular VT) in a patient with Wolff-Parkinson-White pattern
rationale_summary: Wide-complex tachycardia with right bundle branch block morphology and superior axis in a previously healthy 13-year-old with normal echocardiogram is characteristic of fascicular (left ventricular) VT
Adenosine had no effect on the rhythm or ventriculoatrial conduction, arguing against AV node-dependent supraventricular tachycardia

Sinus rhythm after cardioversion showed ventricular pre-excitation with a left bundle branch block morphology that was distinctly different from the tachycardia QRS morphology, supporting VT rather than antidromic AVRT via the accessory pathway
