PMC8904927.diagnosis: Drug-induced myopericarditis (hypersensitivity myocarditis) related to recent lenalidomide/bortezomib therapy
rationale_summary: Symptoms began 19 days after starting lenalidomide/bortezomib/dexamethasone and were preceded by pruritus and a diffuse erythematous pruritic rash

Marked myocardial injury (hs-troponin T 5.47 µg/L) with lateral ST elevation on ECG but no regional wall-motion abnormalities and preserved EF on echocardiography, favoring myocarditis over acute coronary occlusion
Associated localized pericardial effusion supports myopericarditis
Concurrent systemic inflammatory findings (CRP 47.4 mg/L) and elevated CK (2122 U/L) are compatible with a drug hypersensitivity/toxic syndrome with muscle and cardiac involvement

PMC5734493.diagnosis: HIV-associated thrombotic microangiopathy (AIDS-related thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura/hemolytic uremic syndrome)
rationale_summary: Advanced untreated HIV (CD4 19)

No heparin exposure to explain abrupt thrombocytopenia

PMC7174963.diagnosis: Heparin-induced thrombocytopenia with thrombosis (HITT) due to enoxaparin
rationale_summary: Exposure to enoxaparin thromboprophylaxis after knee replacement with symptom onset 15 days later
Marked thrombocytopenia (platelets 43,000 × 10^9/L) confirmed on repeat citrate sample
New, multifocal arterial and venous thromboses: acute ischemic stroke on MRI plus pulmonary emboli, hepatic vein branch thrombosis, and leg DVT
Alternative cardioembolic/atherosclerotic sources not found (sinus rhythm, normal echocardiography, normal carotid duplex)

PMC8133085.diagnosis: Chronic spontaneous urticaria (CSU) temporally associated with vespid (wasp) venom sensitization
rationale_summary: Three weeks of migratory, intensely pruritic wheals lasting <24 hours without identifiable triggers, consistent with CSU

Extensive evaluation for infectious, autoimmune, inflammatory, and coagulation abnormalities is negative/normal (including ESR/CRP, complement, thyroid and celiac antibodies; autologous serum skin test negative)
History of multiple wasp stings two months earlier with acute urticaria and subsequent lymphadenitis, followed by persistent urticaria
Demonstrated vespid sensitization on testing (positive intradermal tests; elevated specific IgE to Vespula spp and rVes v 5)
Symptoms persist despite high-dose antihistamines and prolonged low-dose prednisone, supporting chronic, refractory urticaria rather than an acute allergic reaction

PMC9549187.diagnosis: Giant almost completely thrombosed left renal artery aneurysm causing severe left hydronephrosis
rationale_summary: Six months of worsening left low back pain radiating to the left leg with a palpable non-tender left flank mass
Ultrasound showed a ~100-mm heterogeneous mass adjacent to a calcified aortic wall with minimal Doppler flow
Contrast CT/CT angiography demonstrated a 109×102×94-mm saccular left renal artery aneurysm with mural thrombosis and calcifications with scant contrast filling
Aneurysm compressed the renal pelvis, producing severe hydronephrosis and marked left renal parenchymal thinning (to 2 mm)

PMC5845136.diagnosis: Idiopathic hypereosinophilic syndrome with eosinophilic myocarditis
rationale_summary: Marked hypereosinophilia (AEC 21.6×10^3/µL, 63.5%) with bone marrow eosinophilia and no lymphoproliferative infiltration; FIP1L1-PDGFRA and BCR-ABL negative

Acute cardiac involvement: progressive dyspnea, pulmonary congestion, elevated troponin I (35.4 ng/dL) and BNP (1280.5 pg/mL), sinus tachycardia with widespread ST depressions, global LV hypokinesia with EF 40% and small pericardial effusion
Extensive evaluation for secondary causes of eosinophilia and myocarditis negative (parasites/serologies, HIV/TB, blood cultures, ANA/dsDNA), and no drug/allergy/asthma history
Systemic findings compatible with hypereosinophilic disease (low-grade fever, malaise/sweats, mild hepatosplenomegaly, transaminitis)

PMC5507571.diagnosis: Migrainous infarction (acute ischemic midbrain stroke during migraine with aura)
rationale_summary: History of migraine with visual aura and current episode began with typical pulsatile headache with nausea, photophobia, and phonophobia
Developed focal brainstem signs during the attack (internuclear ophthalmoplegia, dysarthria, imbalance/ataxia)
MRI showed a focal diffusion-restricting lesion with T2/FLAIR hyperintensity in the right dorsal midbrain/medial longitudinal fasciculus, consistent with an acute infarct
CT/CTA were normal and there were no infectious symptoms or other alternative explanations provided

PMC11299065.diagnosis: Tropheryma whipplei (Whipple) endocarditis causing severe aortic and mitral regurgitation with cardiogenic shock
rationale_summary: Known Whipple disease recently confirmed on intestinal biopsy and treated with antibiotics
Presentation with cardiogenic shock, anasarca, and echocardiographic severe aortic and mitral regurgitation with severe right-sided dysfunction
Hemodynamics show severe pulmonary hypertension and elevated wedge pressure consistent with advanced left-sided valvular failure
Blood Whipple PCR negative and endomyocardial biopsy shows no myocardial involvement, pointing away from myocarditis and toward valvular involvement (culture-negative endocarditis pattern)

PMC9721027.diagnosis: Sigmoid volvulus causing large-bowel obstruction complicated by cecal perforation and generalized peritonitis
rationale_summary: Elderly man with progressive colicky abdominal pain, marked distension, persistent vomiting, and absolute constipation consistent with large-bowel obstruction
Prior intermittent left-sided/lower abdominal pain supports a recurrent/intermittent distal colonic volvulus process
Exam showed peritonitis (diffuse tenderness/guarding, absent bowel sounds) and empty rectum
Radiographs showed severe colonic dilation (cecum ≥12 cm) with free subdiaphragmatic air, consistent with pneumoperitoneum from perforation in the setting of distal large-bowel obstruction

Imaging showed new bilateral patchy ground-glass opacities/infiltrates with pleural effusions and severe hypoxemia

Bacteriological tests were negative and echocardiography showed preserved EF with normal wall motion, making infection or cardiogenic edema less likely

rationale_summary: Sigmoid-colon mass composed of uniform small bland cells in sheets/nests with prominent small arterioles and infiltrative growth into muscular wall and mesocolic fat
Immunoprofile supports endometrial stromal differentiation: diffuse CD10+, estrogen receptor+, and progesterone receptor+
Excludes gastrointestinal stromal tumor and other mesenchymal/epithelial tumors: c-kit−, Dog1−, CD34−, SMA−, desmin−, S100−, pan-cytokeratin−
Low-grade behavior supported by absent necrosis/mitoses and low Ki-67 (8%), despite lymphovascular invasion and nodal metastases

PMC11228663.diagnosis: Rhino-orbito-cerebral mucormycosis (acute invasive fungal rhinosinusitis) with angioinvasion causing ophthalmic vessel occlusion, cavernous sinus thrombosis, and cerebral infarctions
rationale_summary: Severely immunocompromised post-allogeneic HSCT with profound neutropenia
Progressive maxillary/ethmoid/sphenoid sinusitis with bony demineralization and contiguous orbital involvement (proptosis, chemosis, ophthalmoplegia, nonreactive pupil)
CTA showed absent opacification of left ophthalmic and facial vessels, consistent with angioinvasive infection and thrombosis
MRI showed restricted-diffusion fronto-basal/temporo-polar infarcts with SWI hypointensities and ischemic signal along the left optic nerve
Nonenhancing left cavernous sinus consistent with cavernous sinus thrombosis
Clinical deterioration despite voriconazole, which does not cover Mucorales

PMC7455415.diagnosis: Anterior mediastinal thymoma (thymic epithelial tumor) mimicking an ectopic parathyroid adenoma
rationale_summary: Resected 18-mm mass was in the anterior mediastinum posterior to the sternum, a typical thymic location
Frozen section showed epithelial sheets/nests with pseudoglandular structures and a patchy peripheral lymphoid component, fitting a thymic epithelial neoplasm rather than parathyroid tissue
Intraoperative PTH failed to fall after excision and instead rose (191.5 pg/mL preop to 149.3 at 10 min then 259.5 at 30 min), arguing the mass was not the hyperfunctioning parathyroid source

PMC8803407.diagnosis: Endogenous Nocardia endophthalmitis (with injection-site infectious scleral necrosis)
rationale_summary: Subacute (2-month) painful blurred vision with severe anterior uveitis, hypopyon, iris nodules, and vitreous opacities on B-scan consistent with chronic infectious endophthalmitis
Routine vitreous stains/cultures and broad PCR (bacterial/fungal/mycobacterial) were negative, fitting difficult-to-detect organisms such as Nocardia

Clinical worsening after starting high-dose systemic prednisolone and development of scleral thinning/melting at injection sites with uveal prolapse support uncontrolled infection with scleral involvement rather than sterile inflammation

PMC6188590.diagnosis: Statin-associated immune-mediated necrotizing myopathy (anti-HMG-CoA reductase-associated IMNM)
rationale_summary: Proximal myalgias/weakness with rising creatine kinase (up to 1400 U/L) temporally associated with statin exposure
CK elevation persisted/rised after stopping atorvastatin and recurred after rechallenge with a different statin (pravastatin)

PMC4840426.diagnosis: Vildagliptin-induced interstitial pneumonia (drug-induced pneumonitis with organizing pneumonia/acute alveolar injury)
rationale_summary: Respiratory symptoms began days after starting vildagliptin and progressed over 2 weeks
CT showed extensive ground-glass opacities with irregular reticulation in a peribronchovascular, basal-dominant distribution with restrictive physiology (FVC 43.2% predicted)
Markedly elevated IgE and BAL eosinophilia/lymphocytosis (12% eosinophils, 57% lymphocytes) with negative microbiology
Biopsy showed subacute interstitial pneumonia with organizing pneumonia and acute alveolar injury plus eosinophilic/lymphoplasmacytic infiltration
No clinical signs of connective-tissue disease and broad autoantibody panel was negative

PMC3914239.diagnosis: Renovascular (malignant) hypertension from left renal artery stenosis in an ectopic intrathoracic kidney, causing advanced hypertensive nephropathy/renal failure
rationale_summary: Severe hypertension with end-organ damage: BP 220/120 mmHg, grade III hypertensive retinopathy, S4/loud A2, pulmonary edema symptoms, reduced urine output and asterixis
Marked renal failure (creatinine 900 µmol/L, urea 30 mmol/L) with proteinuria supports hypertensive/renovascular kidney disease
Ultrasound shows chronic parenchymal damage (loss of corticomedullary differentiation) and small right kidney, suggesting longstanding hypertensive nephropathy
Left kidney is ectopic with an intrathoracic upper margin and Doppler renal artery velocity 195 cm/s consistent with significant renal artery stenosis, supporting renovascular hypertension

PMC6787862.diagnosis: Primary adrenal insufficiency (Addison disease) presenting with adrenal crisis
rationale_summary: Severe hypotonic hyponatremia (Na 101, plasma osmolality 225) with hypovolemia/orthostasis and dehydration
Inappropriately high urine sodium (128) and urine osmolality (128) without diuretic use, consistent with mineralocorticoid deficiency rather than extrarenal salt loss
Symptoms preceding TMP-SMX (1 month fatigue, poor appetite, weight loss, vague abdominal pain) plus new skin tanning/hyperpigmentation support primary adrenal insufficiency
Mild hyperkalemia (K 5.2) and low CO2 (16) support aldosterone deficiency in primary adrenal insufficiency
History of long-standing hypothyroidism suggests autoimmune polyglandular disease including autoimmune adrenalitis

PMC2958974.diagnosis: Sepsis-induced cardiomyopathy (septic myocardial dysfunction) from postpartum Escherichia coli pyelonephritis with acute respiratory failure
rationale_summary: Acute postpartum E. coli urosepsis: fever to 40.5°C, WBC 18,000/µL, costovertebral tenderness, urinalysis with pyuria/hematuria, blood and urine cultures positive for E. coli
Rapid deterioration with hypoxemia (O2 sat 76%), bilateral pleural effusions and bilateral airway disease on imaging, and need for intubation, consistent with severe sepsis/critical illness
New global biventricular hypokinesis with reduced EF (35%) and normal end-diastolic chamber size/normal valves, fitting sepsis-related myocardial depression rather than chronic/dilated cardiomyopathy
No evidence of acute coronary syndrome (normal troponin, ECG without ischemia) and CT negative for pulmonary embolus

PMC8082195.diagnosis: Immune-mediated thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura (TTP) with central nervous system involvement (atypical PRES-like MRI changes)
rationale_summary: Severe thrombocytopenia (7,000/mcL) with diffuse petechial rash
Microangiopathic hemolytic anemia pattern: hemoglobin drop (15.6→10.6 g/dL), elevated LDH (817 U/L), undetectable haptoglobin (<10 mg/dL)
Neurologic manifestations (acute confusion, waxing/waning attention; transient left-arm tingling) with fever
Brain MRI with multifocal T2/FLAIR hyperintense lesions and punctate microhemorrhages without enhancement, compatible with TTP-related encephalopathy/PRES mimic
CSF essentially noninflammatory and broad infectious studies negative, arguing against viral encephalitis
Appropriate empiric treatment initiated for suspected TTP (plasmapheresis and high-dose steroids) with ADAMTS13 activity sent

PMC5015421.diagnosis: Right common peroneal (fibular) neuropathy from compression at the fibular neck due to thromboembolic deterrent stocking

Weak ankle dorsiflexion/eversion and toe extension (1/5) with relative preservation of inversion (4/5), localizing to peroneal nerve distribution
Sensory loss over lateral lower leg and dorsum of foot including first webspace, with preserved plantar sensation and ankle jerk
Well-demarcated stocking indentation at proximal rim exactly at fibular neck on the affected side, supporting external compression

PMC9464768.diagnosis: Isolated torsion of the left fallopian tube
rationale_summary: Sudden onset severe unilateral left lower-quadrant pain with subsequent intermittent symptoms, consistent with torsion/detorsion
Pelvic ultrasound showed a bulky distal left fallopian tube with normal-sized ovaries and normal ovarian Doppler flow, arguing against ovarian torsion or a cyst rupture
Negative pregnancy test and negative urinalysis/vaginal cultures with initially low CRP make ectopic pregnancy and primary PID less likely
Clinical deterioration with fever and markedly rising CRP despite minimal initial findings is compatible with evolving ischemia/necrosis of a torsed tube prompting laparoscopy

PMC10098721.diagnosis: Fatal non-accidental trauma (physical child abuse/battered child syndrome with abusive head injury)
rationale_summary: Postmortem imaging and anthropology showed numerous antemortem fractures: 14 cranial vault fractures plus metaphyseal fractures of the left humerus, radius, and ulna and a remodeled ulnar diaphyseal fracture with rotation
Fractures were in multiple healing stages on gross exam and histology (fibrous tissue/capillary ingrowth to woven bone/cartilage), indicating repeated injuries over time

Associated soft-tissue/cranial findings (scalp hemorrhages over skull fractures, hemorrhagic CSF, focal subacute subarachnoid hemorrhage) support inflicted head trauma rather than a single accidental event

PMC11442918.diagnosis: Intestinal tuberculosis (ileocecal/colonic TB mimicking Crohn's disease, with evidence of pulmonary TB)
rationale_summary: Steroid-refractory 'Crohn's' flare with subsequent high fever, severe anemia, and profound hypoalbuminemia
CT showed calcified mediastinal/hilar nodes and right middle-lobe calcified atelectasis plus widespread intra-abdominal lymphadenopathy and ascites, noted atypical for Crohn's and suggestive of TB

Repeat colonoscopy lacked longitudinal ulcers/cobblestoning and instead showed circumferential transverse ulcers, patulous ileocecal valve with pseudodiverticular deformity, and rectosigmoid sparing-features favoring intestinal TB over Crohn's
Origin from Myanmar (higher TB prevalence) and prior negative IGRA obtained under steroids (possible false-negative)

PMC8828200.diagnosis: Chronic enteropathy associated with SLCO2A1 (chronic nonspecific multiple ulcers of the small intestine)
rationale_summary: Extensive, map-like shallow ulcers with well-defined margins and residual mucosal islands localized to the terminal ileum, with prior capsule endoscopy showing multiple ileal aphtha-like erosions
Biopsies showed only nonspecific lymphocytic infiltration without malignancy or cytomegalovirus
Terminal ileal ulcers persisted unchanged 1 month after discontinuation of NSAIDs while upper-GI erosions/ulcers healed with PPI
Infectious and systemic inflammatory causes were not supported (negative stool cultures, negative MPO/PR3-ANCA and ANA, unremarkable contrast CT)

PMC5470563.diagnosis: Juvenile granulosa cell tumor of the testis (congenital testicular sex cord-stromal tumor)
rationale_summary: Presented at birth with isolated unilateral testicular enlargement/mass and otherwise normal external genitalia
Scrotal ultrasound showed an intratesticular solid mass without retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy
Serum AFP was normal for gestational age, arguing against a yolk sac (germ cell) tumor in this setting

PMC4898367.diagnosis: Primary sebaceous carcinoma of the parotid gland
rationale_summary: Parotid mass with carcinoma showing lobulated nests of large foamy/vacuolated clear cells with central nuclei, rimmed by smaller basaloid cells-morphology typical of sebaceous differentiation
Associated squamous islands with keratin pearl formation can be seen in sebaceous carcinoma
Foamy cells are PAS-negative, arguing against glycogen- or mucin-rich clear cell tumors (e.g., mucoepidermoid/clear cell carcinomas)

PMC5632850.diagnosis: TIPIC syndrome (transient perivascular inflammation of the carotid artery; carotidynia)
rationale_summary: Acute focal left neck pain maximal over the carotid bifurcation, worsened by palpation and neck movement, with normal neurologic exam
CT/MRI show eccentric perivascular soft-tissue thickening around distal common carotid/carotid bulb/proximal ICA with intense enhancement
Doppler shows wall thickening but normal intraluminal flow and no intimal dissection or aneurysm; MRI shows no wall hematoma
No involvement of other supra-aortic vessels/aortic arch; abdominal MRI excludes fibromuscular dysplasia/vasculitis
Inflammatory markers and serologies largely unremarkable (normal WBC/CRP, only minimally elevated ESR; negative ANA/ANCA)

PMC9446685.diagnosis: Transient constrictive pericarditis after cardiac surgery (post-pericardiotomy constriction)
rationale_summary: Four weeks post-CABG and bioprosthetic AVR with new dyspnoea, orthopnoea/PND, and progressive oedema
Markedly elevated JVP with positive Kussmaul's sign and peripheral oedema suggesting right-sided heart failure physiology

Echocardiography shows bright thickened pericardium with small effusion, dilated IVC without inspiratory collapse, septal bounce (ventricular interdependence), and marked respiratory variation of mitral (>25%) and tricuspid (>40%) inflow velocities consistent with constrictive physiology
Normal LV systolic function and normal prosthetic valve function argue against primary myocardial or valvular cause

PMC5953188.diagnosis: Congenital pulmonary airway malformation (CPAM), type 1 (formerly congenital cystic adenomatoid malformation) of the right lung with secondary left lung hypoplasia
rationale_summary: Prenatal ultrasound and autopsy show a large multicystic mass occupying the right hemithorax with mediastinal shift and reduced left thoracic volume
Right lung contains multiple contiguous large cysts (up to 7 cm), typical of CPAM type 1
Histology shows cysts lined by ciliated pseudostratified epithelium with mucous (goblet cell) clusters and mucinous glands, supporting type 1 CPAM
Left lung is small/immature with mostly collapsed alveoli, consistent with secondary pulmonary hypoplasia from mass effect

PMC3574658.diagnosis: Pseudohypoparathyroidism type 1A (Albright hereditary osteodystrophy with hormone resistance due to GNAS defect)
rationale_summary: Early-onset severe obesity with hyperphagia and round facies/central obesity consistent with Albright hereditary osteodystrophy phenotype
Persistently elevated PTH despite normal calcium and phosphate suggests evolving PTH resistance
Repeated elevated TSH with normal free T4 and confirmed mild primary hypothyroidism indicates TSH resistance, which commonly co-occurs with PTH resistance in pseudohypoparathyroidism type 1A
History of mild developmental delay is compatible with this disorder

Chest radiograph: enlarged cardiac silhouette plus bilateral pleural effusions

PMC7924147.diagnosis: Disseminated histoplasmosis (Histoplasma capsulatum) with bilateral adrenal involvement causing primary adrenal insufficiency
rationale_summary: Advanced untreated HIV (CD4 27/μL) predisposing to disseminated fungal infection
Biopsy of maxillary mass showed granulomatous sheets with macrophages containing well-demarcated fungal spores consistent with intracellular Histoplasma
Systemic findings (weight loss, cough, hilar lymphadenopathy, rash) with bilateral adrenal enlargement on CT

Primary adrenal insufficiency (hyponatremia, mild hyperkalemia, elevated ACTH with minimal cortisol rise to cosyntropin) and negative 21-hydroxylase antibodies supporting infectious adrenalitis rather than autoimmune disease

PMC5005770.diagnosis: Chronic mefloquine neurotoxicity (mefloquine-induced neuropsychiatric syndrome with central vestibulopathy)
rationale_summary: Symptoms (anxiety, vivid dreams) began within 2 weeks of starting weekly mefloquine prophylaxis and progressed while he continued the drug for 6 months

Persistent long-term neuropsychiatric complaints (insomnia with vivid dreams/nightmares, irritability, cognitive/word-finding and memory difficulties) and vestibular symptoms ("sea legs," constant spinning dizziness, frequent vertigo with falls) years after exposure
Vestibular testing showed a central vestibulopathy (hyperactive VOR gains and low phase on rotary chair) with normal MRI/IAC imaging and negative infectious/toxicologic workup
Enemy gunfire trauma occurred after symptom onset and did not change symptoms, making a primary PTSD/trauma etiology less consistent with the time course

PMC10618618.diagnosis: Mucinous morphea (localized scleroderma) associated with IgG kappa monoclonal gammopathy
rationale_summary: Indurated dusky patches on trunk and a firm plaque on calf clinically consistent with localized sclerosing dermatosis
Biopsies show dermal sclerosis with hyalinized collagen extending into subcutis and diminished/absent CD34+ dermal dendrocytes in sclerotic zones, supporting morphea
Back biopsy shows abundant interstitial dermal mucin (colloidal iron positive) within sclerotic dermis, consistent with mucinous morphea
Persistent IgG kappa monoclonal gammopathy and no systemic mucinosis on CT help exclude systemic mucinous disorders (e.g., scleromyxedema)

PMC9803558.diagnosis: Postictal diffuse alveolar hemorrhage (seizure-associated DAH/negative-pressure pulmonary hemorrhage)

Chest CT showed bilateral diffuse ground-glass opacities consistent with diffuse alveolar hemorrhage/pulmonary edema; prior similar imaging after a seizure
Prior bronchoscopy during a similar post-seizure episode showed frank blood in the airways

PMC10065398.diagnosis: Unilateral subacute (de Quervain) thyroiditis causing destructive thyrotoxicosis

Thyrotoxicosis with suppressed TSH and elevated free T4/T3 plus markedly elevated thyroglobulin and negative thyroid antibodies, consistent with destructive thyroiditis rather than Graves

Inflammatory markers elevated (CRP 38.5 mg/L, mild leukocytosis) and ultrasound shows hypoechoic inhomogeneous right lobe with decreased vascularity (left lobe normal), fitting subacute thyroiditis with unilateral involvement
Rapid symptomatic improvement after starting high-dose prednisolone supports subacute thyroiditis

PMC8853404.diagnosis: COVID-19-associated mononeuritis multiplex (acute bilateral tibial and common peroneal mononeuropathies)
rationale_summary: New focal lower-limb weakness (marked left ankle dorsiflexion 2/5) with absent knee/ankle reflexes and otherwise normal neurologic exam, favoring peripheral nerve involvement

Electrodiagnostics show asymmetric, multifocal lower-extremity nerve involvement: absent left superficial peroneal sensory response, bilateral peroneal motor slowing with absent F waves, tibial CMAP reduction with slowed velocities and absent F waves, absent H reflexes

Needle EMG demonstrates acute denervation (fibrillations/positive sharp waves) in peroneal-innervated muscles bilaterally with reduced recruitment, with normal upper-extremity studies, supporting multiple acute lower-limb mononeuropathies rather than generalized polyneuropathy

rationale_summary: Recurrent left-sided cervical abscesses often triggered by upper respiratory infections, suggesting an aerodigestive tract communication
Multiple prior surgeries and repeated CT/MRI/barium studies failed to show a typical second branchial cleft cyst/fistula, and original pathology showed pseudoepithelium rather than true cyst lining
Associated dysphagia and dysphonia with recurrent deep neck infection pattern is characteristic of third/fourth branchial arch anomalies, most commonly a left fourth-arch pyriform sinus tract

PMC7968274.diagnosis: Intraluminal (intrasinus) meningioma of the right sigmoid sinus causing secondary intracranial hypertension
rationale_summary: Bilateral papilledema with markedly elevated CSF opening pressure (500 mmH2O) and normal CSF composition indicates intracranial hypertension
Head CT showed no mass lesion or hydrocephalus, prompting evaluation for venous outflow obstruction
MRI demonstrated a 2.5 cm right sigmoid sinus lesion that was isointense on T1/T2 and homogeneously enhancing-typical imaging behavior of meningioma
CT venography showed subtotal occlusion of the dominant right sigmoid sinus by a well-defined intraluminal mass, explaining the raised intracranial pressure

PMC10860510.diagnosis: Idiopathic myointimal hyperplasia of the mesenteric veins (IMHMV)
rationale_summary: Middle-aged man with rectosigmoid-predominant colitis/stricture on colonoscopy and CT (bowel wall thickening from splenic flexure to rectum with luminal narrowing)

CT shows venous abnormalities (thin cord-like inferior mesenteric vein with ectatic mesenteric venous branches) while the inferior mesenteric artery is patent and there is no thrombosis or arteriosclerosis
Endoscopic biopsies show only mild mucosal inflammation without features supporting inflammatory bowel disease or eosinophilic enteritis, fitting a venous outflow-related ischemic colitis mimic

PMC11101242.diagnosis: Delusional infestation (Morgellons disease / delusional parasitosis) presenting as perceived ocular foreign bodies
rationale_summary: 15-year fixed belief that diverse "foreign bodies" (plant parts, fibers, hair, thread, metal, insects) are emanating from the superior fornices despite repeated normal ophthalmic examinations with lid eversion and sweeping
Collected and presented self-extracted debris over years (matchbox-like behavior) and performed repetitive extraction rituals
Orbital radiograph and MRI were normal; no lesions, ulcers, masses, or retained foreign bodies were seen on multiple evaluations

Symptoms (burning, dryness, redness, pain, blurred vision) with only mild objective findings (trace injection, punctate corneal staining, floppy eyelid syndrome) insufficient to explain reported material extrusion
Mental status on presentation was organized and cooperative with intact orientation and cognition, consistent with a circumscribed somatic delusion

PMC6076939.diagnosis: Posterior vaginal wall epidermal inclusion cyst (post-obstetric trauma) with secondary inflammation/infection
rationale_summary: Mass first noted at vacuum-assisted vaginal delivery and remained stable for years, consistent with a post-traumatic inclusion cyst
Posterior vaginal wall/rectovaginal septum location, well-circumscribed smooth cystic lesion ~4-5 cm
New serosanguinous/watery discharge and dyspareunia with overlying draining exophytic area suggests secondary inflammation/rupture
Biopsy showed squamous debris with acute/chronic inflammation, supporting an epidermal inclusion cyst
MRI showed a complex cystic mass with wall enhancement and no internal enhancement superiorly, compatible with an inflamed cyst rather than a solid tumor

Recurrent exertional episodes of proximal muscle weakness are consistent with episodic symptomatic hypokalemia

PMC2892661.diagnosis: Cervical spondylotic myeloradiculopathy (cervical spinal cord/nerve root compression mimicking ALS)
rationale_summary: Progressive upper-limb weakness with atrophy and fasciculations plus extremely brisk reflexes suggests combined lower and upper motor neuron involvement from cervical cord/root compression
Neck pain with arm numbness/clumsiness, hyper-/dysesthesia over shoulders/proximal arms, and reduced vibration sense in all limbs indicate sensory pathway involvement, arguing against ALS
Gait ataxia with preserved bulbar function and flexor plantar responses is consistent with cervical myelopathy rather than motor neuron disease

PMC3638553.diagnosis: Chronic inflammatory demyelinating polyneuropathy (CIDP) with respiratory muscle involvement causing chronic hypercapnic respiratory failure
rationale_summary: Three months of progressively worsening symmetric proximal and distal limb weakness with hyporeflexia
Associated distal sensory loss (decreased pinprick) supports a peripheral demyelinating polyneuropathy
Chronic respiratory insufficiency with hypercapnia (pCO2 58 mm Hg) and hypoxemia without lung disease or heart failure on imaging/BNP/echo suggests neuromuscular ventilatory failure
Course is chronic (>8 weeks) and progressive, fitting CIDP rather than acute Guillain-Barré syndrome

PMC6864292.diagnosis: Idiopathic hypereosinophilic syndrome (HES) with thrombotic cutaneous necrosis and pulmonary involvement
rationale_summary: Marked persistent hypereosinophilia (21.15×10^9/L; 57.8%) with bone marrow eosinophilia (27.5%)
Secondary causes (allergic, parasitic, rheumatologic, immunologic) reported unremarkable
Evidence of end-organ involvement: progressive thigh lesion to large cutaneous necrosis; bilateral pulmonary opacities with hemoptysis/chest pain; elevated liver enzymes
Coagulation/thrombotic features supporting eosinophilia-associated thrombosis: very high D-dimer (82.20 mg/L FEU) with severe thrombocytopenia

PMC9449604.diagnosis: Pembrolizumab-induced immune-related pancreatitis (autoimmune pancreatitis-like, mass-forming pancreatitis)
rationale_summary: Temporal association: pancreatic mass/ductal changes developed 3 months after stopping long-term pembrolizumab
Imaging favored inflammatory pancreatitis/AIP-like process: pancreatic head enlargement with delayed enhancement, multiple main pancreatic duct stenoses, and intrapancreatic bile duct stenosis
EUS showed hypoechoic parenchyma with scattered hyperechoic foci/stranding consistent with pancreatitis
Pancreatic juice, bile, and bile-duct brushing cytology were negative for malignancy
Serum IgG4 was normal, making classic IgG4-related autoimmune pancreatitis less likely and supporting an immune-checkpoint-inhibitor-related etiology

PMC11497400.diagnosis: Takotsubo syndrome (stress-induced cardiomyopathy) presenting as STEMI mimic
rationale_summary: Elderly woman with acute dyspnea and ECG ST-segment elevations with modest troponin rise
Transthoracic echocardiography showed akinesis of the apical mid-segments (typical stress cardiomyopathy pattern)

Emergency coronary angiography did not identify a culprit lesion matching the anterior/apical wall-motion abnormality (only focal mid-RCA stenosis), supporting a STEMI mimic such as Takotsubo syndrome

PMC1971059.diagnosis: Primary carcinoid tumor (well-differentiated neuroendocrine tumor) of skeletal muscle (right sartorius)
rationale_summary: Slowly enlarging intramuscular thigh mass with histology of polygonal cells in packets separated by delicate fibrovascular stroma
Neuroendocrine differentiation: strong diffuse synaptophysin, chromogranin, and neuron-specific enolase positivity; electron microscopy shows abundant neurosecretory granules
Epithelial nature supports carcinoid/neuroendocrine tumor: diffuse cytokeratin positivity; excludes myogenic and other mimics (desmin-, S100-, EMA-)
Biochemical support for serotonin-secreting carcinoid: elevated 24-hour urinary 5-HIAA
No evidence of another primary on CT, MIBG, or octreotide scans, supporting a primary soft-tissue neuroendocrine tumor rather than metastasis

PMC4356045.diagnosis: Swyer-James-MacLeod syndrome (postinfectious unilateral hyperlucent lung with associated bronchiectasis/air-trapping)
rationale_summary: Young never-smoker with no toxic exposures and normal α1-antitrypsin/immunologic workup, making smoking-related diffuse emphysema unlikely
Chest radiograph shows unilateral increased lucency of the left hemithorax; CT shows extensive unilateral bullae/cysts occupying nearly the entire left hemithorax with ipsilateral bronchiectasis

Physiology suggests marked air-trapping/ventilation inhomogeneity (RV 156% predicted; TLC low by nitrogen washout but near-normal by plethysmography), consistent with a unilateral hyperlucent, air-trapping lung syndrome
Bronchoscopy without malignancy or granulomas supports a non-neoplastic, non-granulomatous airway/destructive process

PMC1079925.diagnosis: False-positive somatostatin receptor scintigraphy uptake from poststernotomy inflammatory/granulation tissue (no mediastinal carcinoid recurrence)
rationale_summary: He had extensive surgical resection for thymic atypical carcinoid with sternotomy and vascular grafting, and early postoperative CT documented poststernotomy changes.
At 1-year follow-up, CT showed no mediastinal recurrence despite the mediastinal focus on In-111 pentetreotide scintigraphy.
Discordance between negative anatomic imaging (no recurrent mass) and focal octreotide uptake supports an inflammatory/postsurgical cause rather than residual/recurrent tumor.

PMC6120420.diagnosis: Pembrolizumab (immune checkpoint inhibitor)-associated myocarditis causing cardiogenic shock with secondary ischemic acute tubular necrosis
rationale_summary: On pembrolizumab for metastatic lung adenocarcinoma

Severe hypotension (SBP 50-70 mm Hg) with new systolic dysfunction (EF 55-60% → 35%) and elevated troponin (2.8 ng/mL) without regional wall-motion abnormalities, supporting myocarditis/cardiac inflammatory injury over ischemic MI
Marked AKI (creatinine 7.2 mg/dL from 1.0-1.2) with FeUrea 46% and bland urinalysis (minimal protein, no pyuria/casts), supporting intrinsic injury consistent with ischemic ATN
No hydronephrosis on ultrasound and no new nephrotoxin/contrast exposure, making obstruction or drug/contrast nephrotoxicity less likely

PMC11526588.diagnosis: Richter transformation of chronic lymphocytic leukemia to an aggressive lymphoma (most consistent with diffuse large B-cell lymphoma)
rationale_summary: Known 8-year history of CLL with new rapid clinical deterioration (progressive bulky lymphadenopathy, intermittent fevers, weight loss).
PET/CT shows very intense FDG uptake in nodal and nasopharyngeal/tonsillar disease (SUVmax ~18-21), which is concerning for transformation rather than indolent CLL.
Airway-compromising nasopharyngeal/tonsillar mass with widespread FDG-avid lymphadenopathy supports an aggressive lymphomatous process arising in the setting of CLL.

PMC11842074.diagnosis: Inferomedial orbital dermoid cyst (congenital inclusion cyst) with acute inflammation
rationale_summary: 21-year-old with inferomedial medial canthal orbital mass causing superotemporal globe displacement and mild adduction limitation
Acute pain/erythema with preseptal cellulitis improved on antibiotics while a firm swelling persisted/progressed, consistent with inflamed/ruptured cyst

CT showed a large heterogeneous radiolucent cystic lesion with an internal radiopaque solid component and attachment near the inferior orbital rim/lacrimal sac fossa-typical of a dermoid at a bony suture with calcified contents
Lacrimal irrigation was patent and there was no punctal discharge, arguing against primary lacrimal sac pathology despite lifelong epiphora

PMC10941813.diagnosis: Giant prostatic leiomyoma (benign smooth muscle tumor arising from the prostate/bladder neck)

PMC9463427.diagnosis: Primary synovial sarcoma of the right axilla
rationale_summary: Right axillary mass was a discrete, well-defined soft-tissue tumor without surrounding lymphadenopathy or chest-wall invasion, and was FDG-avid (SUVmax 5.1).

PMC7374193.diagnosis: Bilateral juxtapapillary retinal capillary hemangioblastomas (optic nerve head hemangioblastomas), von Hippel-Lindau-like but genetic testing negative
rationale_summary: 15-year history of bilateral optic nerve head swelling with suspected optic disc masses, initially misdiagnosed as pseudotumor cerebri and unresponsive to optic nerve sheath fenestration
Progressive optic nerve head lesions extending into the papillomacular bundles with severe bilateral vision loss
Managed for years with intravitreal anti-VEGF (bevacizumab/aflibercept) and steroid implants with limited response, consistent with exudative vascular optic disc tumors
Whole exome sequencing negative for VHL and related syndromic genes despite a phenotype typical of retinal/optic nerve head hemangioblastomas

PMC9650838.diagnosis: Autoimmune glial fibrillary acidic protein (GFAP) astrocytopathy presenting as recurrent meningoencephalitis with intracranial hypertension
rationale_summary: Decade-long recurrent episodes of fever, headache, vomiting and impaired consciousness with meningeal signs
CSF repeatedly showed marked pleocytosis with very high protein and low glucose, yet initial CSF cultures and metagenomic sequencing were negative for pathogens
Brain MRI showed symmetric periventricular/brainstem/internal capsule white-matter hyperintensities consistent with an inflammatory meningoencephalitic process

rationale_summary: Abrupt onset (within 1-2 minutes) immediately after starting IV diclofenac infusion

Improvement after stopping diclofenac and administering adrenaline with supportive treatment

PMC6029059.diagnosis: Hepatic veno-occlusive disease (sinusoidal obstruction syndrome) after autologous hematopoietic stem-cell transplantation

PMC7904273.diagnosis: Celiac disease (gluten-sensitive enteropathy) with associated aplastic anemia/pancytopenia
rationale_summary: 6 months of chronic diarrhea and abdominal pain with declining weight z-score, hypoalbuminemia, and elevated ESR/CRP with negative stool infectious studies

Upper endoscopy showed duodenal mucosal congestion/edema with otherwise normal colon and terminal ileum, supporting a proximal small-bowel process such as celiac disease (tTG IgA and duodenal biopsy pending)

Severe neutropenia, anemia with very low reticulocyte percentage, and mild thrombocytopenia with hypocellular bone marrow and normal iron/B12/folate/copper/zinc argue for aplastic anemia rather than nutritional deficiency or malignancy

PMC4184604.diagnosis: Bilateral chronic exertional compartment syndrome of the lower legs
rationale_summary: Many-year history of bilateral leg pain that worsens with activity and causes nocturnal cramps relieved by walking
Marked diffuse tenderness over all leg muscle compartments bilaterally, increased after a 3-minute exertional step test
Normal arterial pulses and no venous insufficiency signs, arguing against vascular claudication
Lumbar imaging and nerve conduction studies normal, making radiculopathy/large-fiber neuropathy less likely
Localization of sensory reduction below the knees in deep/superficial fibular and sural distributions is consistent with exertional compartment-related nerve compression

PMC11264972.diagnosis: Azathioprine-induced hypersensitivity syndrome (sepsis-mimicking drug reaction with distributive shock and myocardial injury)
rationale_summary: Symptoms began within a week of starting azathioprine (fever, abdominal pain, persistent tachycardia/hypotension progressing to distributive shock and renal failure).
Extensive infectious workup (CXR, blood/urine cultures, CT A/P) found no source despite broad-spectrum antibiotics.
Developed acute erythematous pustular rash during the illness (biopsied).
Single azathioprine re-challenge triggered rapid recurrence of vasodilatory shock within 12 hours, strongly implicating azathioprine as the cause.

Re-challenge was accompanied by transient ST elevations and marked troponin rise with normal coronary angiography, consistent with hypersensitivity-related myocardial involvement rather than obstructive CAD.

PMC9296717.diagnosis: Abdominal wall myofascial pain syndrome (internal oblique muscle trigger point) after laparoscopic hepatectomy
rationale_summary: Severe localized right upper abdominal wall pain provoked by movement/posture with positive Carnett's sign, supporting an abdominal wall (not visceral) source
Exam showed a hypersensitive tender spot with a taut band and palpation reproduced radiating epigastric pain, meeting myofascial trigger-point criteria
Pain was temporarily relieved by local anesthetic (mepivacaine) injection into the tender spot

Ultrasound demonstrated a focal intramuscular hyperechoic spot with surrounding hypoechoic change within the internal oblique, with intact muscle fiber continuity and location not along a vertical nerve course or port-site discontinuity, favoring a trigger point over nerve entrapment/port-site injury

PMC1808459.diagnosis: Fibromatosis-like (low-grade spindle cell) metaplastic carcinoma of the breast
rationale_summary: Infiltrative bland spindle-cell tumor with prominent keloid-like sclerosis and invasion into adjacent fat
Diffuse epithelial marker expression (cytokeratin, CK7, high-molecular weight CK, EMA) despite sarcomatoid morphology, with coexpression of vimentin
Basal/myoepithelial-associated markers only focal (p63, smooth muscle actin) and low proliferative index (Ki-67 <5%)
Lack of ER/PR/HER2 and absence of DCIS or overt squamous differentiation supports low-grade metaplastic spindle cell carcinoma over primary sarcoma or fibromatosis

PMC9289430.diagnosis: Hypokalemic paralysis due to distal renal tubular acidosis (type 1 RTA)
rationale_summary: Rapidly progressive flaccid quadriparesis with areflexia but normal sensation and cranial nerves
Severe hypokalemia (K+ 1.72 mEq/L; ABG K+ 1.38 mEq/L) sufficient to cause paralysis, ileus (distended abdomen, absent bowel sounds), and respiratory failure
Concurrent metabolic acidosis with low HCO3− (pH 7.12, HCO3− 12.5 mmol/L) and otherwise normal renal profile supports renal tubular acidosis rather than Guillain-Barré syndrome
ECG changes (ST depression, T-wave inversion) consistent with severe hypokalemia

PMC7424302.diagnosis: Iatrogenic acute septal perforator (septal branch of LAD) coronary artery injury/vasospasm from left bundle branch pacing lead implantation causing transient anterior STEMI
rationale_summary: Acute chest pain, diaphoresis, and hypotension occurred during deep septal lead advancement with impedance rise then abrupt fall (suggesting tissue disruption/trauma)
ECG showed ST-segment elevation in I, aVL, and V1-V3 with reciprocal inferior ST depression, consistent with acute anterior/anterolateral ischemia
Urgent angiography showed a large septal branch at the initial lead site (anatomic proximity to the screwed-in lead) with preserved LAD TIMI 3 flow
Immediate lead withdrawal/repositioning led to rapid (<10 min) resolution of symptoms and ST changes, supporting transient septal branch compromise rather than fixed LAD occlusion

PMC11524424.diagnosis: Myxedema coma (severe decompensated hypothyroidism from levothyroxine nonadherence)
rationale_summary: History of congenital hypothyroidism with cessation of levothyroxine for months and severe malnutrition/weight loss
Presentation with progressive confusion/unconsciousness plus hypothermia (33.1 °C), hypotension, and borderline bradycardia-classic for myxedema coma
Laboratory evidence of severe hypothyroidism: TSH 71 μIU/mL with very low free T4 (0.23 ng/dL) and free T3 (0.96 pg/mL)
Associated hyponatremia (Na 127 mmol/L) consistent with severe hypothyroidism
No clear alternative cause found on imaging/toxicology; cortisol normal and no evidence for infection, malignancy, DIC/MAHA, or HLH to explain the encephalopathy/shock physiology

PMC11256461.diagnosis: Sulfasalazine-induced DRESS syndrome (drug reaction with eosinophilia and systemic symptoms)
rationale_summary: Temporal relationship: systemic illness and rash began after ~3 weeks of starting sulfasalazine and progressed despite delayed withdrawal
Typical clinical features: febrile morbilliform/blanching pruritic rash with facial/pharyngeal swelling and tender lymphadenopathy
Hematologic findings: leukocytosis with eosinophilia (rising to 11%)
Internal organ involvement: hepatitis (AST/ALT elevation), hypoalbuminemia/INR elevation, serositis/effusions (pericardial, pleural) and massive ascites with hepatosplenomegaly

Extensive generalized lymphadenopathy on CT and intensely FDG-avid nodes on PET compatible with DRESS-associated lymphadenopathy; major infections and alternative causes were investigated/considered, and he met all 7 RegiSCAR criteria

PMC7441664.diagnosis: Hirayama disease (juvenile distal upper-limb monomelic amyotrophy)
rationale_summary: Young man with insidious, predominantly distal upper-limb weakness and hand atrophy (first dorsal interosseous) with brachioradialis sparing and no sensory/autonomic symptoms
Lower motor neuron signs (atrophy, reduced tone, fasciculations/minipolymyoclonus) without clear upper motor neuron signs and with preserved gait/cranial nerves
Electrodiagnostics show motor axonal involvement and active/chronic denervation in C7-T1-innervated muscles (FDI; right biceps/triceps) with normal sensory exam
Typical natural history: progression for a few years followed by stabilization; cervical MRI (including flexion) can be normal despite the clinical/EMG pattern

PMC3920376.diagnosis: In-flight barotrauma from a giant left apical pulmonary bulla (altitude-related expansion causing transient pleuritic chest pain with left arm weakness)
rationale_summary: Recurrent, predictable episodes occur only at cruising altitude and fully resolve on descent, indicating a cabin-pressure-related mechanism
Symptoms are pleuritic left-sided chest pain radiating to the back, consistent with pleural/stretch pain from an apical lesion under changing pressure
CT pulmonary angiography shows a solitary large left apical bulla (10×8×8 cm), providing a structural air space that can expand at reduced cabin pressure
Normal examination, oxygen saturation, spirometry, lung volumes, transfer factor, and normal head MRI make persistent cardiopulmonary disease or central neurologic causes less likely

PMC10377245.diagnosis: Opioid toxicity with opioid-induced rigidity (naloxone-responsive postoperative opioid effect)
rationale_summary: Developed sudden unresponsiveness with rigidity/extensor posturing and fixed pinpoint pupils shortly after receiving intraoperative fentanyl and hydromorphone
Rapid, complete return of responsiveness after repeated small-dose naloxone strongly supports opioid effect
Recurrent episode later is consistent with renarcotization from residual opioid exposure; head CT was unremarkable and there was no fever, clonus, or hypoxia to suggest alternative diagnoses

Rapid clinical worsening and increase in lesion size over ~10 days supports an aggressive process such as lymphoma

PMC6935604.diagnosis: Takotsubo (stress-induced) cardiomyopathy triggered during EPOCH chemotherapy
rationale_summary: Baseline echocardiogram was normal (EF 65%) before chemotherapy
Within 10 hours of starting doxorubicin/etoposide/vincristine infusion she acutely developed hypoxia and pulmonary edema with enlarged cardiac silhouette
ECG showed new T-wave inversions; biomarkers showed very high NT-proBNP (7000 pg/mL) with only modest troponin rise (peak 0.7 ng/mL)

Bedside echocardiogram showed apical ballooning with severe anterior/anteroseptal hypokinesis and reduced EF (~20%), a pattern characteristic of Takotsubo rather than chronic/global anthracycline cardiomyopathy
Required inotropic support (dobutamine) consistent with acute stress cardiomyopathy causing cardiogenic shock

PMC10682226.diagnosis: Well-differentiated cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma (facial SCC)
rationale_summary: Progressive 3-year facial plaques/nodules with ulceration/crusting and tissue destruction compromising vision

Multiple infectious evaluations repeatedly negative (fungi/mycobacteria stains/cultures, Giemsa, Leishmania PCR) and no durable response to antibiotics, antifungals, antimonials, antituberculous therapy, or corticosteroids

Nasal skin biopsy showed malignant squamous features: prominent epidermal hyperplasia with atypia, increased mitoses, invasion of squamous nests, and keratin pearls-diagnostic of well-differentiated SCC
Actinic cheilitis on upper lip biopsy supports UV-related squamous neoplasia; staging showed no metastases

PMC3113998.diagnosis: Hypercapnic cerebral edema with increased intracranial pressure (CO2 narcosis) from acute-on-chronic hypercapnic respiratory failure
rationale_summary: Arterial blood gas on controlled ventilation showed severe hypercapnia and acidosis (pH 7.22, pCO2 85 mmHg) with elevated bicarbonate (33 mEq/L) suggesting chronic CO2 retention with an acute rise
Head CT demonstrated diffuse signs of increased intracranial pressure (bilateral effaced sulci, small ventricles, narrowed basilar cisterns)

Neurologic findings (unresponsiveness, fixed dilated right pupil, blurred disc margins) are consistent with markedly elevated intracranial pressure/herniation physiology in the setting of severe hypercapnia

PMC7423524.diagnosis: Cryptogenic multifocal ulcerous stenosing enteritis (CMUSE) causing recurrent small-bowel ulceration and stricture with incomplete obstruction
rationale_summary: 9-month relapsing abdominal pain with incomplete intestinal obstruction (reduced defecation, multiple air-fluid levels on X-ray, segmental dilatation on CT)
Enteroscopy showed a small-intestinal ulcer with luminal stenosis, supporting ulcerating stricturing small-bowel disease
Inflammatory markers and cultures were unremarkable (normal ESR/CRP/procalcitonin; negative blood cultures), making active infection less likely
Symptoms improved with high-dose corticosteroids but obstructive symptoms persisted/relapsed with taper, consistent with steroid-responsive ulcerative stenosing enteritis
